# [Climate, Justice, and the world Katrina Left Behind](https://public-api.wordpress.com/bar/?stat=groovemails-events&bin=wpcom_email_click&redirect_to=http%3A%2F%2Fgeojones.org%2F2025%2F08%2F30%2Fkatrina20years%2F&sr=0&signature=272ed0a9cde8e3a56d2b8e267d3c0a5a&blog_id=26166017&user=248418217&_&_z=z)
| | By **Rebekah Jones** on August 30, 2025
---|---
[](https://geojones2.wordpress.com?action=user_content_redirect&uuid=fe03b804ed89c48e4d20b5ac060f161a4c421c2b243178ed322c270cdf2323d3&blog_id=26166017&post_id=3635&user_id=248418217&subs_id=487468588&signature=238a0c2d283ed2ced684bad33db66334&email_name=new-post&user_email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&encoded_url=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cubWVzb3NjYWxlbmV3cy5jb20vc3Vic2NyaWJl&email_id=47d5a9d9b2580b4dbac7a794702543a7)
You’re receiving this email because you have been an engaged reader of my newsletter,[ **Mesoscale News**](https://geojones2.wordpress.com?action=user_content_redirect&uuid=e859777a0766bcf80df0600bfa14a27b45e225e5b22cc345a7fb78792010888c&blog_id=26166017&post_id=3635&user_id=248418217&subs_id=487468588&signature=64907116837bae82bbcd55b47c413b72&email_name=new-post&user_email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&encoded_url=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cubWVzb3NjYWxlbmV3cy5jb20v&email_id=47d5a9d9b2580b4dbac7a794702543a7). A recent error deleted most of my subscriber list, and I hope you'll re-subscribe and help me to rebuild my network. [**Click here to subscribe (free).**](https://geojones2.wordpress.com?action=user_content_redirect&uuid=fe03b804ed89c48e4d20b5ac060f161a4c421c2b243178ed322c270cdf2323d3&blog_id=26166017&post_id=3635&user_id=248418217&subs_id=487468588&signature=238a0c2d283ed2ced684bad33db66334&email_name=new-post&user_email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&encoded_url=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cubWVzb3NjYWxlbmV3cy5jb20vc3Vic2NyaWJl&email_id=47d5a9d9b2580b4dbac7a794702543a7)
[Click here to read this post on Mesoscale News and re-subscribe](https://geojones2.wordpress.com?action=user_content_redirect&uuid=fe03b804ed89c48e4d20b5ac060f161a4c421c2b243178ed322c270cdf2323d3&blog_id=26166017&post_id=3635&user_id=248418217&subs_id=487468588&signature=238a0c2d283ed2ced684bad33db66334&email_name=new-post&user_email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&encoded_url=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cubWVzb3NjYWxlbmV3cy5jb20vc3Vic2NyaWJl&email_id=47d5a9d9b2580b4dbac7a794702543a7)
* * *
At 11:10 AM CT on August 29, 2005, 120 mph (200 km/hour) winds battered the Mississippi coastline, bringing a record-breaking 28 foot storm surge with it, breaking the earlier record held by Hurricane Camille (1969) by almost four feet.
At 11:10 AM CT on August 29, 2005, I was 16 years old, dancing in the wind and rain of Hurricane Katrina as she made her second landfall at the Mississippi-Alabama border.
[](https://geojones2.wordpress.com?action=user_content_redirect&uuid=50395f138d7d6f7e09bd7bbd346fc5c034c2b4378fe7eeb034abd4f4faa258c6&blog_id=26166017&post_id=3635&user_id=248418217&subs_id=487468588&signature=0469d3a7c3580f1dfb60ba64f580cbf2&email_name=new-post&user_email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&encoded_url=aHR0cHM6Ly9zdWJzdGFja2Nkbi5jb20vaW1hZ2UvZmV0Y2gvJTI0c18hTXlyZyEsZl9hdXRvLHFfYXV0bzpnb29kLGZsX3Byb2dyZXNzaXZlOnN0ZWVwL2h0dHBzJTNBJTJGJTJGc3Vic3RhY2stcG9zdC1tZWRpYS5zMy5hbWF6b25hd3MuY29tJTJGcHVibGljJTJGaW1hZ2VzJTJGMDZkYjI5MDEtYjg1Yi00NTNlLWExMmItMjk0MTVjZWQyN2E1XzUwNHgzMzYuanBlZw=&email_id=47d5a9d9b2580b4dbac7a794702543a7)
Twenty years ago from the moment I published this piece, my life and the lives of everyone from the region were irrevocably changed.
Katrina didn’t just arrive and vanish; it settled in, reshaping lives, communities, the whole country. I was there, and in many ways, I’m still there. The storm lingers, like a high water line haunting the soul.
I have spoken in depth about the storm’s impact on me, my family and the communities that raised me.
For all of those who forgot, didn’t pay attention, or were not yet born, I hope this summary will provide a grounded understanding of what Katrina means to survivors like me.
[Click here to subscribe](https://geojones2.wordpress.com?action=user_content_redirect&uuid=fe03b804ed89c48e4d20b5ac060f161a4c421c2b243178ed322c270cdf2323d3&blog_id=26166017&post_id=3635&user_id=248418217&subs_id=487468588&signature=238a0c2d283ed2ced684bad33db66334&email_name=new-post&user_email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&encoded_url=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cubWVzb3NjYWxlbmV3cy5jb20vc3Vic2NyaWJl&email_id=47d5a9d9b2580b4dbac7a794702543a7)
Because to survive a storm like Katrina is to never truly leave it behind. The water may drain, the houses may be rebuilt, the debris hauled off—but the storm nests inside you. I’ve said before that Katrina was one of the few events that shifted the very axis of my life, alongside the birth of my son and the raid on my home.
Survival does not mean escape. It means carrying the wound, waking to it when the sky darkens or the air presses heavy, recognizing it in every new storm alert that buzzes across your phone. People assume trauma softens with time, but sometimes it calcifies into identity—you become the storm’s witness, its archivist, the one who can’t forget even when the world insists on forgetting.
* * *
# A Quiet Morning, A Shattered World
On a late August morning in 2005, the Earth weaponized the ocean against us.
The day had an eerie calm to it at first — quiet and calm enough to wrongly assume they had overplayed the monster lurching toward us. By mid-morning, the reality of what was upon us became clear.
[Click here to subscribe](https://geojones2.wordpress.com?action=user_content_redirect&uuid=fe03b804ed89c48e4d20b5ac060f161a4c421c2b243178ed322c270cdf2323d3&blog_id=26166017&post_id=3635&user_id=248418217&subs_id=487468588&signature=238a0c2d283ed2ced684bad33db66334&email_name=new-post&user_email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&encoded_url=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cubWVzb3NjYWxlbmV3cy5jb20vc3Vic2NyaWJl&email_id=47d5a9d9b2580b4dbac7a794702543a7)
I remember how disarming it felt as the day remained sunny through the entire storm. I’ve seen the sky go black, suddenly, threateningly before severe weather rolled through. But not on this day.
That day the sun hung above it all, almost mocking, as if indifferent to the havoc unleashed beneath its steady gaze. That dissonance—the calm light paired with the storm’s rage— felt like a trick. It felt less like weather and more like a reminder: the world is not bound to behave by our expectations, and it doesn’t owe us warning signs.
Katrina was, at the time, a once-in-a-lifetime beast. In the modern record, only three landfalls carried comparable fury: the Great Galveston Hurricane of 1900, the Labor Day Hurricane of 1935, and Camille in 1969. Roughly every thirty-five years, it seemed, the Gulf of Mexico demanded tribute in total devastation. Katrina felt like the next terrible installment in that grim cycle.
But the climate we inhabit now has broken those patterns. The ocean is warmer—globally by about 1.5°F (0.85°C) since the late 19th century, and the Gulf of Mexico is heating even faster, setting record sea surface temperatures nearly every summer. Warmer seas are jet fuel for cyclones, and warmer air holds more water, which translates into storms that are both more intense and more flood-laden.
[Click here to subscribe](https://geojones2.wordpress.com?action=user_content_redirect&uuid=fe03b804ed89c48e4d20b5ac060f161a4c421c2b243178ed322c270cdf2323d3&blog_id=26166017&post_id=3635&user_id=248418217&subs_id=487468588&signature=238a0c2d283ed2ced684bad33db66334&email_name=new-post&user_email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&encoded_url=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cubWVzb3NjYWxlbmV3cy5jb20vc3Vic2NyaWJl&email_id=47d5a9d9b2580b4dbac7a794702543a7)
That’s why Katrina was followed so quickly by Rita and Wilma, both stronger at peak intensity and arriving within mere weeks. It’s why Harvey in 2017 dumped more than 60 inches of rain on Texas—rainfall scientists have shown was made at least three times more likely by climate change.
It’s why Laura and Ida, fifteen and sixteen years after Katrina, blasted Louisiana with winds nearly as powerful, while Michael in 2018 tore into the Florida Panhandle as the strongest storm to ever strike that coastline. The “once-in-a-lifetime” scale has collapsed; storms that should be rare are now relentless.
The numbers tell the same story survivors already know in their bones. Since 2005, the Atlantic has produced more Category 5 hurricanes than in the entire first half of the 20th century combined. Between 1980 and 2010, only three Atlantic storms rapidly intensified by more than 70 mph in a single day; between 2017 and 2021, we saw ten.
This is the signature of a planet running a fever: storms that form stronger, intensify faster, and linger longer. Survivors of Katrina recognize the echo in every new storm that rises from overheated waters—the lesson that what once felt exceptional is becoming the norm in a climate unmoored.
* * *
### A Social Catastrophe
The Galveston Hurricane of 1900 remains the deadliest natural disaster in American history, with as many as 12,000 lives lost. Yet even then, much of the death toll came not from nature’s fury alone, but from human arrogance. Warnings had come from Cuban meteorologists that a monster storm was bearing down, but U.S. forecasters dismissed them as unreliable. The city’s leaders, eager to preserve commerce, told residents to stay calm. By the time the Gulf rose and the winds ripped homes apart, there was nowhere to run.
[Click here to subscribe](https://geojones2.wordpress.com?action=user_content_redirect&uuid=fe03b804ed89c48e4d20b5ac060f161a4c421c2b243178ed322c270cdf2323d3&blog_id=26166017&post_id=3635&user_id=248418217&subs_id=487468588&signature=238a0c2d283ed2ced684bad33db66334&email_name=new-post&user_email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&encoded_url=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cubWVzb3NjYWxlbmV3cy5jb20vc3Vic2NyaWJl&email_id=47d5a9d9b2580b4dbac7a794702543a7)
This pattern repeats across the centuries. Time and again, the storm itself is only half the story. The Labor Day Hurricane of 1935, the most intense to ever strike U.S. soil, killed more than 400 people—many of them World War I veterans sent to the Florida Keys to build a highway. They were housed in flimsy work camps with no real evacuation plan, despite forecasts of a major storm. Government negligence, not just the hurricane, sealed their fate.
Katrina followed the same script, written in even larger letters. The levees in New Orleans—designed and built by human hands—collapsed under pressure they should have withstood. Evacuation orders came late and without provisions for the poor, the elderly, or those without cars. Thousands were left to suffer in the Superdome or on rooftops as the water rose. The storm surge was monstrous, yes, but the real catastrophe was the scaffolding of decisions, delays, and dismissals that turned a natural disaster into a human one.
And still, we’ve failed to learn. When Hurricane Harvey poured more than 60 inches of rain over Texas in 2017, it was climate change that loaded the clouds, but it was human development—paving over wetlands, ignoring floodplain maps—that left Houston defenseless. In 2018, Hurricane Michael exploded into a Category 5 in the Gulf, but what doomed so many in Mexico Beach was not ignorance of its strength, but the absence of strict building codes and a culture of denial that told people “it can’t happen here.”
[Click here to subscribe](https://geojones2.wordpress.com?action=user_content_redirect&uuid=fe03b804ed89c48e4d20b5ac060f161a4c421c2b243178ed322c270cdf2323d3&blog_id=26166017&post_id=3635&user_id=248418217&subs_id=487468588&signature=238a0c2d283ed2ced684bad33db66334&email_name=new-post&user_email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&encoded_url=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cubWVzb3NjYWxlbmV3cy5jb20vc3Vic2NyaWJl&email_id=47d5a9d9b2580b4dbac7a794702543a7)
What survivors know is this: storms will come, and they will grow stronger as the planet warms. That much is out of our hands. But the choices we make—the strength of our levees, the honesty of our warnings, the justice of our evacuation plans—are ours to own. The deadliest hurricanes in American history are less indictments of the weather than of ourselves. The lesson is simple, though rarely heeded: the storm is merciless, but the greater danger is our refusal to listen, to prepare, to value every life equally before the water rises.
What Katrina revealed wasn’t just vulnerability to storms — it was the geography of injustice, etched long before the winds picked up.
In New Orleans’ Lower Ninth Ward, 98% African American at the time, floodwaters rose quickly after the levees failed. Hundreds drowned in attics, unable to hack through their roofs. Those who survived faced years of bureaucratic neglect: federal housing programs after Katrina systematically gave white homeowners more aid than Black homeowners for the same amount of damage, according to a 2010 HUD investigation. Renters — disproportionately Black and poor — were often left out entirely.
In Gulfport, Mississippi, homeowners with insurance rebuilt within a year. Just blocks away, Black families without policies — or with coverage denied under the label of “storm surge” rather than “wind” — remained in FEMA trailers for half a decade. Casinos returned faster than schools.
Disasters, geographers remind us, do not create inequality. They expose it.
* * *
### The First Climate Refugees
Perhaps the most haunting legacy of Katrina is [Isle de Jean Charles](https://geojones2.wordpress.com?action=user_content_redirect&uuid=e3f8d88addb8f280d00920801e15e96f9124c43f40e0b694c8f37f5e0cb3731b&blog_id=26166017&post_id=3635&user_id=248418217&subs_id=487468588&signature=b9c13d76c78c5515d0e8f60fd6badda8&email_name=new-post&user_email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&encoded_url=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuaXNsZWRlamVhbmNoYXJsZXMuY29tLw=&email_id=47d5a9d9b2580b4dbac7a794702543a7). Once a thriving island community of several hundred, it has lost 98% of its land since 1955. After Katrina, repeated storms finally forced a reckoning: the tribe became America’s first official climate refugees, offered government-built subdivisions 40 miles inland from what supposed to be miracle HUD funding.
[Click here to subscribe](https://geojones2.wordpress.com?action=user_content_redirect&uuid=fe03b804ed89c48e4d20b5ac060f161a4c421c2b243178ed322c270cdf2323d3&blog_id=26166017&post_id=3635&user_id=248418217&subs_id=487468588&signature=238a0c2d283ed2ced684bad33db66334&email_name=new-post&user_email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&encoded_url=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cubWVzb3NjYWxlbmV3cy5jb20vc3Vic2NyaWJl&email_id=47d5a9d9b2580b4dbac7a794702543a7)
But relocation is not the same as justice. Families were uprooted from ancestral fishing grounds. Burial sites are now underwater or collapsing into the rising water table. Children grow up without the marshes that defined their parents’ lives. It is not just land that is lost, but culture, sovereignty, memory.
For Isle de Jean Charles, home to the Biloxi-Chitimacha-Choctaw, Katrina was one of many storms that made staying impossible. Within a decade, the community would accept the first federally recognized climate relocation in the U.S. The federal government paid $48 million to move families inland. It was called “resettlement.” For the tribe, it was exile.
I worked on the HUD grant for the Isle de Jean Charles. I was there when the state took the money and gave it to developers with connections to both the awarding agency and their politicians. I was there during the public meeting when Chief Albert Naquin closed his eyes to keep from crying as the new proposal site was approved — without any input from the tribe itself. All of that work, effort, and worst of all hope, only for the state bureaucracy to squander and steal it.
[Click here to subscribe](https://geojones2.wordpress.com?action=user_content_redirect&uuid=fe03b804ed89c48e4d20b5ac060f161a4c421c2b243178ed322c270cdf2323d3&blog_id=26166017&post_id=3635&user_id=248418217&subs_id=487468588&signature=238a0c2d283ed2ced684bad33db66334&email_name=new-post&user_email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&encoded_url=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cubWVzb3NjYWxlbmV3cy5jb20vc3Vic2NyaWJl&email_id=47d5a9d9b2580b4dbac7a794702543a7)
What happened there is not an isolated story. It is the leading edge of a national trend. The Union of Concerned Scientists estimates that by 2100, 13 million Americans could be displaced by sea-level rise. Katrina’s displaced communities were the first. They will not be the last.
“We lost more than houses,” Houma elder Russell Dupre once said. “We lost our map to who we are.”
* * *
### The Storm That Never Ends
Katrina should have been the breaking point, the storm so devastating it forced America to change course. But the years after told a different story.
Carbon emissions kept rising. Coastal wetlands continued vanishing at the rate of a football field every 100 minutes in Louisiana. Oil and gas drilling expanded. Flood insurance programs were patched, not transformed. And FEMA — politicized and weakened — became once again a pawn of ideology rather than science.
[Click here to subscribe](https://geojones2.wordpress.com?action=user_content_redirect&uuid=fe03b804ed89c48e4d20b5ac060f161a4c421c2b243178ed322c270cdf2323d3&blog_id=26166017&post_id=3635&user_id=248418217&subs_id=487468588&signature=238a0c2d283ed2ced684bad33db66334&email_name=new-post&user_email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&encoded_url=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cubWVzb3NjYWxlbmV3cy5jb20vc3Vic2NyaWJl&email_id=47d5a9d9b2580b4dbac7a794702543a7)
We rebuilt what had been destroyed, often in the same vulnerable places, without addressing why it was destroyed in the first place. We raised levees higher around New Orleans, but ignored the sinking land beneath them. We reopened casinos faster than we rebuilt schools. We treated Katrina as a one-time tragedy, instead of the first chapter of a new climate era.
For survivors, Katrina is not past tense. It is present. It lives in the memories of children who grew up in FEMA trailers, in the tribal elders who mourn lost land, in the Mississippi families who never came home.
And it should live in all of us — as a warning. Because Katrina was not just a storm; it was a prophecy. And we are living in its shadow still.
The Gulf is hotter. The storms are faster. The waters are rising. The injustice remains.
The storm that never ends is not Katrina itself. It is our refusal to listen to what Katrina was trying to tell us.
[Click here to subscribe](https://geojones2.wordpress.com?action=user_content_redirect&uuid=fe03b804ed89c48e4d20b5ac060f161a4c421c2b243178ed322c270cdf2323d3&blog_id=26166017&post_id=3635&user_id=248418217&subs_id=487468588&signature=238a0c2d283ed2ced684bad33db66334&email_name=new-post&user_email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&encoded_url=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cubWVzb3NjYWxlbmV3cy5jb20vc3Vic2NyaWJl&email_id=47d5a9d9b2580b4dbac7a794702543a7)
_In loving memory and in grief of all the lives lost and forever changed on August 29, 2005._

Click here to subscribe (fre... | or | | | or ---|---|--- | | By Rebekah Jones on August 30, 2025 ---|--- You’re receiving this email because you have been an engaged reader of my newsletter,. A recent error deleted most of my subscriber list, and I hope you'll re-subscribe and help me to rebuild my network. At 11:10 AM CT on August 29, 2005, 120 mph (200 km/hour) winds battered the Mississippi coastline, bringing a record-breaking 28 foot storm surge with it, breaking the earlier record held by Hurricane Camille (1969) by almost four feet. At 11:10 AM CT on August 29, 2005, I was 16 years old, dancing in the wind and rain of Hurricane Katrina as she made her second landfall at the Mississippi-Alabama border. Twenty years ago from the moment I published this piece, my life and the lives of everyone from the region were irrevocably changed. Katrina didn’t just arrive and vanish; it settled in, reshaping lives, communities, the whole country. I was there, and in many ways, I’m still there. The storm lingers, like a high water line haunting the soul. I have spoken in depth about the storm’s impact on me, my family and the communities that raised me. For all of those who forgot, didn’t pay attention, or were not yet born, I hope this summary will provide a grounded understanding of what Katrina means to survivors like me. Because to survive a storm like Katrina is to never truly leave it behind. The water may drain, the houses may be rebuilt, the debris hauled off—but the storm nests inside you. I’ve said before that Katrina was one of the few events that shifted the very axis of my life, alongside the birth of my son and the raid on my home. Survival does not mean escape. It means carrying the wound, waking to it when the sky darkens or the air presses heavy, recognizing it in every new storm alert that buzzes across your phone. People assume trauma softens with time, but sometimes it calcifies into identity—you become the storm’s witness, its archivist, the one who can’t forget even when the world insists on forgetting. A Quiet Morning, A Shattered World On a late August morning in 2005, the Earth weaponized the ocean against us. The day had an eerie calm to it at first — quiet and calm enough to wrongly assume they had overplayed the monster lurching toward us. By mid-morning, the reality of what was upon us became clear. I remember how disarming it felt as the day remained sunny through the entire storm. I’ve seen the sky go black, suddenly, threateningly before severe weather rolled through. But not on this day. That day the sun hung above it all, almost mocking, as if indifferent to the havoc unleashed beneath its steady gaze. That dissonance—the calm light paired with the storm’s rage— felt like a trick. It felt less like weather and more like a reminder: the world is not bound to behave by our expectations, and it doesn’t owe us warning signs. Katrina was, at the time, a once-in-a-lifetime beast. In the modern record, only three landfalls carried comparable fury: the Great Galveston Hurricane of 1900, the Labor Day Hurricane of 1935, and Camille in 1969. Roughly every thirty-five years, it seemed, the Gulf of Mexico demanded tribute in total devastation. Katrina felt like the next terrible installment in that grim cycle. But the climate we inhabit now has broken those patterns. The ocean is warmer—globally by about 1.5°F (0.85°C) since the late 19th century, and the Gulf of Mexico is heating even faster, setting record sea surface temperatures nearly every summer. Warmer seas are jet fuel for cyclones, and warmer air holds more water, which translates into storms that are both more intense and more flood-laden. That’s why Katrina was followed so quickly by Rita and Wilma, both stronger at peak intensity and arriving within mere weeks. It’s why Harvey in 2017 dumped more than 60 inches of rain on Texas—rainfall scientists have shown was made at least three times more likely by climate change. It’s why Laura and Ida, fifteen and sixteen years after Katrina, blasted Louisiana with winds nearly as powerful, while Michael in 2018 tore into the Florida Panhandle as the strongest storm to ever strike that coastline. The “once-in-a-lifetime” scale has collapsed; storms that should be rare are now relentless. The numbers tell the same story survivors already know in their bones. Since 2005, the Atlantic has produced more Category 5 hurricanes than in the entire first half of the 20th century combined. Between 1980 and 2010, only three Atlantic storms rapidly intensified by more than 70 mph in a single day; between 2017 and 2021, we saw ten. This is the signature of a planet running a fever: storms that form stronger, intensify faster, and linger longer. Survivors of Katrina recognize the echo in every new storm that rises from overheated waters—the lesson that what once felt exceptional is becoming the norm in a climate unmoored. A Social Catastrophe The Galveston Hurricane of 1900 remains the deadliest natural disaster in American history, with as many as 12,000 lives lost. Yet even then, much of the death toll came not from nature’s fury alone, but from human arrogance. Warnings had come from Cuban meteorologists that a monster storm was bearing down, but U.S. forecasters dismissed them as unreliable. The city’s leaders, eager to preserve commerce, told residents to stay calm. By the time the Gulf rose and the winds ripped homes apart, there was nowhere to run. This pattern repeats across the centuries. Time and again, the storm itself is only half the story. The Labor Day Hurricane of 1935, the most intense to ever strike U.S. soil, killed more than 400 people—many of them World War I veterans sent to the Florida Keys to build a highway. They were housed in flimsy work camps with no real evacuation plan, despite forecasts of a major storm. Government negligence, not just the hurricane, sealed their fate. Katrina followed the same script, written in even larger letters. The levees in New Orleans—designed and built by human hands—collapsed under pressure they should have withstood. Evacuation orders came late and without provisions for the poor, the elderly, or those without cars. Thousands were left to suffer in the Superdome or on rooftops as the water rose. The storm surge was monstrous, yes, but the real catastrophe was the scaffolding of decisions, delays, and dismissals that turned a natural disaster into a human one. And still, we’ve failed to learn. When Hurricane Harvey poured more than 60 inches of rain over Texas in 2017, it was climate change that loaded the clouds, but it was human development—paving over wetlands, ignoring floodplain maps—that left Houston defenseless. In 2018, Hurricane Michael exploded into a Category 5 in the Gulf, but what doomed so many in Mexico Beach was not ignorance of its strength, but the absence of strict building codes and a culture of denial that told people “it can’t happen here.” What survivors know is this: storms will come, and they will grow stronger as the planet warms. That much is out of our hands. But the choices we make—the strength of our levees, the honesty of our warnings, the justice of our evacuation plans—are ours to own. The deadliest hurricanes in American history are less indictments of the weather than of ourselves. The lesson is simple, though rarely heeded: the storm is merciless, but the greater danger is our refusal to listen, to prepare, to value every life equally before the water rises. What Katrina revealed wasn’t just vulnerability to storms — it was the geography of injustice, etched long before the winds picked up. In New Orleans’ Lower Ninth Ward, 98% African American at the time, floodwaters rose quickly after the levees failed. Hundreds drowned in attics, unable to hack through their roofs. Those who survived faced years of bureaucratic neglect: federal housing programs after Katrina systematically gave white homeowners more aid than Black homeowners for the same amount of damage, according to a 2010 HUD investigation. Renters — disproportionately Black and poor — were often left out entirely. In Gulfport, Mississippi, homeowners with insurance rebuilt within a year. Just blocks away, Black families without policies — or with coverage denied under the label of “storm surge” rather than “wind” — remained in FEMA trailers for half a decade. Casinos returned faster than schools. Disasters, geographers remind us, do not create inequality. They expose it. The First Climate Refugees Perhaps the most haunting legacy of Katrina is . Once a thriving island community of several hundred, it has lost 98% of its land since 1955. After Katrina, repeated storms finally forced a reckoning: the tribe became America’s first official climate refugees, offered government-built subdivisions 40 miles inland from what supposed to be miracle HUD funding. But relocation is not the same as justice. Families were uprooted from ancestral fishing grounds. Burial sites are now underwater or collapsing into the rising water table. Children grow up without the marshes that defined their parents’ lives. It is not just land that is lost, but culture, sovereignty, memory. For Isle de Jean Charles, home to the Biloxi-Chitimacha-Choctaw, Katrina was one of many storms that made staying impossible. Within a decade, the community would accept the first federally recognized climate relocation in the U.S. The federal government paid $48 million to move families inland. It was called “resettlement.” For the tribe, it was exile. I worked on the HUD grant for the Isle de Jean Charles. I was there when the state took the money and gave it to developers with connections to both the awarding agency and their politicians. I was there during the public meeting when Chief Albert Naquin closed his eyes to keep from crying as the new proposal site was approved — without any input from the tribe itself. All of that work, effort, and worst of all hope, only for the state bureaucracy to squander and steal it. What happened there is not an isolated story. It is the leading edge of a national trend. The Union of Concerned Scientists estimates that by 2100, 13 million Americans could be displaced by sea-level rise. Katrina’s displaced communities were the first. They will not be the last. “We lost more than houses,” Houma elder Russell Dupre once said. “We lost our map to who we are.” The Storm That Never Ends Katrina should have been the breaking point, the storm so devastating it forced America to change course. But the years after told a different story. Carbon emissions kept rising. Coastal wetlands continued vanishing at the rate of a football field every 100 minutes in Louisiana. Oil and gas drilling expanded. Flood insurance programs were patched, not transformed. And FEMA — politicized and weakened — became once again a pawn of ideology rather than science. We rebuilt what had been destroyed, often in the same vulnerable places, without addressing why it was destroyed in the first place. We raised levees higher around New Orleans, but ignored the sinking land beneath them. We reopened casinos faster than we rebuilt schools. We treated Katrina as a one-time tragedy, instead of the first chapter of a new climate era. For survivors, Katrina is not past tense. It is present. It lives in the memories of children who grew up in FEMA trailers, in the tribal elders who mourn lost land, in the Mississippi families who never came home. And it should live in all of us — as a warning. Because Katrina was not just a storm; it was a prophecy. And we are living in its shadow still. The Gulf is hotter. The storms are faster. The waters are rising. The injustice remains. The storm that never ends is not Katrina itself. It is our refusal to listen to what Katrina was trying to tell us. In loving memory and in grief of all the lives lost and forever changed on August 29, 2005. ABOUT REBEKAH an award-winning and internationally celebrated scientist turned whistleblower currently serving as the lead Climate and Ocean Science Advisor to , a UN-backed NGO, and on the . Rebekah studied earth science with a dual major in journalism at Syracuse University for her undergraduate degree, as well as her Master of Science degree at Louisiana State University, and her doctoral studies at Florida State University. Rebekah’s work tracking COVID-19 in Florida earned international praise, and her advocacy for data access and transparency won her Forbes’ Technology Person of the Year and a nomination for Nature’s John Maddox Prize. Rebekah now heads , an independent science newsletter covering international crises, climate change, natural disasters, politics, and whistleblowing. You can and , where she’s amassed nearly one million followers and consistently reaches more than 10 million people each month. * Florida residents save 20%. You must use a Florida billing address when making your payment to qualify. name: Ruben Gallego
subject: Never expected this.
disclaimer_text: Paid For By Gallego for Arizona
body:
I never would have thought that the people who stormed the Capitol that day, who violently assaulted police officers, would go free two years later.
#

Hey, Peter.
While I was on patrol as a Marine in Iraq, while my brothers and I were in combat, while my friends were being wounded and killed... I never would’ve expected that nearly 20 years later I’d have to be ready to fight to defend our democracy on the floor of the House of Representatives.
And as I stood on the House floor on January 6th, teaching my colleagues how to use gas masks, texting my staff on where to hide and how to keep safe, getting ready to defend myself... I never would have thought that the people who stormed the Capitol that day, who violently assaulted police officers, would go free two years later.
But on his first day in office, President Trump pardoned and commuted sentences for every single one of the January 6th rioters. And that was just the start.
In the eight months since, Trump illegally disbanded independent agencies aimed at holding his White house accountable. Ignored direct orders from the Supreme Court. Arrested judges. Deployed the Marines and the National Guard against American cities. Gave billionaires a tax cut and took health care from hundreds of millions of people.
It's a mess. And it's alright to be worried.
Here’s something I believe, though: This country is stronger than the actions of any one person, even the president. And if we stick together, we’ll make it through this.
I’ve always been lucky to have friends, family, and a community that had my back. I would not have been able to build a life for myself without them. I would not have gotten into Harvard, or survived Iraq without them.
I am serving in the United States Senate to deliver for them — and for you.
[**If you’re with me, it’d mean a lot if you could chip in $5 or $10 to my campaign. We’ve got a lot of work to do to fight back, and donations like this are how we keep up the pressure.**](https://act.gallegoforarizona.com/go/1000?c0=ruben-em-aug25.JsYhtJ&refcode=em250830-1-f&t=1&akid=3953%2E734936%2EehcOZ0)
_If you 've stored your info with ActBlue, we'll process your contribution instantly._

name: Beta males
subject: A chance to emasculate Elon and the Tate bros (again)
body:
The proceeds of a violent human trafficking operation that enslaved hundreds of young women are being invested into taking Pennsylvania so far to the right it will be unrecognizable.
| | | | | [](https://yfq78zhbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001Vjr6ErUoNmjuvY0lcpAej_uzS6U5iw8_Ds6Vp8qopoUgDNnpOQpaB4hPiskYK_PRIR0ReYX6PM9tXWVSXmpNWffrdhTOaSk61vNX2MGId47N5aaG5ho6cNQ0h_bXbhhFpICe5c5eJEEsJn7t-R9nbvNTQgq_gKt9jMuE1PseCPCpny2LRBNPMvPCozmHVz1uWCdCkO2ZuXPYpkl5mn7hmQVWmWx7F-lW&c=D0EiwMlYxiUJC0auHaBFHmJV6gIo-pmMcxlJtCRw43xUp3HywR0RUA==&ch=wogZIwEdU67eZ3nfnf6DRY3X3zK2WuRNb7CwfOrufgHhRKH1mq0eKQ==)
---
| Peter,
In Spring 2025, Wisconsin held a high stakes election to determine which party would control the key swing state's Supreme Court.
Trump handpicked Elon Musk to quarterback the GOP's efforts and it went as you would expect - the GOP effort imploded like a Space-X rocket. In fact, Elon flubbed so badly that it led to his falling out with the President.
Now, Elon has joined with "masculine" influencer and human trafficker Tristan Tate, brother of Andrew Tate, to flip Pennsylvania's Supreme Court in a high stakes Nov. 2025 election. If they win, all of PA's blue congressional seats will be redistricted, a full abortion ban will be upheld, and they can rig election '28 in PA to ensure a third Trump term.
---
| | [](https://yfq78zhbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001Vjr6ErUoNmjuvY0lcpAej_uzS6U5iw8_Ds6Vp8qopoUgDNnpOQpaB4hPiskYK_PRIR0ReYX6PM9tXWVSXmpNWffrdhTOaSk61vNX2MGId47N5aaG5ho6cNQ0h_bXbhhFpICe5c5eJEEsJn7t-R9nbvNTQgq_gKt9jMuE1PseCPCpny2LRBNPMvPCozmHVz1uWCdCkO2ZuXOMoIDASdNIPxjJSSOa3Fo_&c=D0EiwMlYxiUJC0auHaBFHmJV6gIo-pmMcxlJtCRw43xUp3HywR0RUA==&ch=wogZIwEdU67eZ3nfnf6DRY3X3zK2WuRNb7CwfOrufgHhRKH1mq0eKQ==)
---
| The dark money PAC that Tate and Musk are bankrolling to steal the Pennsylvania Supreme Court this November.
---
| Tristan Tate's involvement in this race means that the proceeds of a violent human trafficking operation that enslaved hundreds of young women are being invested into taking Pennsylvania so far to the right it will be unrecognizable.
Tate and Musk are both extreme misogynists and they are working hard to ensure that abortion is banned in Pennsylvania.
Sadly, the massive infusions of money they have pumped into this race has given the GOP a massive edge, catapulting the Republicans into the lead whilst Democrats are nowhere to be found. We are on track for disaster.
Defend our Courts is working to salvage this race. There are still 66 days remaining until this key election -- enough time for Democrats to turn things around, mount a resistance to the GOP's full court press, and emasculate the two beta males bankrolling the GOP's efforts here.
[Can we count on you to rush a donation of whatever you can spare to help save the Democrats on this critical court and ensure that these evil men don't win?](https://yfq78zhbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001Vjr6ErUoNmjuvY0lcpAej_uzS6U5iw8_Ds6Vp8qopoUgDNnpOQpaB4hPiskYK_PRIR0ReYX6PM9tXWVSXmpNWffrdhTOaSk61vNX2MGId47N5aaG5ho6cNQ0h_bXbhhFpICe5c5eJEEsJn7t-R9nbvNTQgq_gKt9jMuE1PseCPCpny2LRBNPMvPCozmHVz1uWCdCkO2ZuXO-DpOgySN3Yb4bzzhzjHHM&c=D0EiwMlYxiUJC0auHaBFHmJV6gIo-pmMcxlJtCRw43xUp3HywR0RUA==&ch=wogZIwEdU67eZ3nfnf6DRY3X3zK2WuRNb7CwfOrufgHhRKH1mq0eKQ==)

name: Elect State Dems (via DLCC.org)
subject: If you are a Democrat, please keep reading:
disclaimer_text: paid for by DLCC Victory Fund, 1225 Eye Street NW Suite 1250, Washington, DC 20005
body:
If every supporter reading this donated to support state Dems, we’d have the resources to defend and expand Democratic power from right-wing extremists in state legislatures across the map. | | |
---
| |
---|---

| 🫏 If you are a local Democrat, please continue reading.

State Democrats are facing competitive special elections over the next few months.

name: Mark Warner
subject: I’ll be completely honest: your donation today will have a much bigger impact than a donation of the same size a year from now.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Kaine for Virginia
body:
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em-cgp-2025-warner [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em-cgp-2025-warner]
Derek, for my entire career, I’ve been focused on where we’re headed, on what’s next.
Some of you may know I was an early investor in the cell phone industry... I always remember friends of mine telling me, “Warner, you're crazy,” but I was thinking about what was on the horizon.
When I was first elected governor it was on the promise to move Virginia forward. And we did.
And in the Senate, I’ve been focused on the challenges our country faces next: rebuilding our nation’s infrastructure for a 21st century economy, strengthening cybersecurity, modernizing national security operations, and investing in our communities.
So on this campaign, I’ve got to think about what’s next, too.
November 2026 may seem far away, but the reality is the decisions my campaign makes this summer will have an enormous impact on the outcome of the midterms in Virginia. We’ve got to decide how many staff to bring on, the scale of the advertising campaigns we’ll run and when to start, and so much more.
That’s why Tim Kaine asked me to reach out to you.
Will you split a contribution between my campaign and Tim's Common Ground PAC today as an investment in the early infrastructure we're building? Your donation today will have a much bigger impact than a donation of the same size a year from now. [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em-cgp-2025-warner]
If you’ve saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:
DONATE $25 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em-cgp-2025-warner?express_lane=true&amount=25]
DONATE $50 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em-cgp-2025-warner?express_lane=true&amount=50]
DONATE $100 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em-cgp-2025-warner?express_lane=true&amount=100]
DONATE OTHER [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em-cgp-2025-warner]
Thank you,
Mark Warner
CONTRIBUTE [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em-cgp-2025-warner]
Paid for by Kaine for Virginia
1490-5A Quarterpath Road #272
Williamsburg, VA 23185

body:

Hakeem Jeffries is the Democratic Leader of the U.S. House of Representatives. Like many of us, Hakeem was raised by a working-class family; His mom was a social worker and his dad was a substance abuse counselor.

Now, as the top Democrat in the House, he’s fighting to stop MAGA extremism and reclaim the majority for Democrats.

Hakeem is committed to electing a record number of Democrats in the next election and beyond. But he can’t compete with Republicans’ special interest cash without our support. name: The Democratic Party
subject: Republicans don’t want your voice to count
body:
---
Derek, Right now, Republicans across the country are working to replicate the redistricting done in Texas. They know their best chance of winning in 2026 is by drawing districts that favor themselves. And if you’re a Democrat, _they don’t want your voice to count._ **Democrats are fighting back — but this is an all-hands-on-deck moment, and we’re asking for your help.** Here at the DNC, we are using every tool at our disposal to protect our democracy: We’re taking Trump to court, and investing _big_ in organizing programs to make sure folks have all of the information they need to cast their votes this year and beyond. Thousands of grassroots donors have pitched in to help fund work like this — and today’s the day we’re asking you to join them. [**Our average online contribution right now is $26. Can you match that today to help us fight back against Trump’s attempts to dismantle our democracy?**](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em-dnc-august-2025?amount=26&refcode=em_20250830_4453_fr&refcode2=4453_22930333_v83_cR&akid=4453%2E22930333%2Ev83_cR) _If you 've stored your info with ActBlue, we'll process your contribution instantly:_ | | | **[CONTRIBUTE $26](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em-dnc-august-2025?express_lane=true&amount=26&refcode=em_20250830_4453_fr&refcode2=4453_22930333_v83_cR&akid=4453%2E22930333%2Ev83_cR)**
---
****
| **[CONTRIBUTE OTHER](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em-dnc-august-2025?refcode=em_20250830_4453_fr&refcode2=4453_22930333_v83_cR&akid=4453%2E22930333%2Ev83_cR)**
---
****
We are committed to continuing our work to organize and mobilize against Trump while electing Democrats at every level of government — leaders who will check Trump’s power in their cities, districts, and states.
We can only do this with your help. [ **With our democracy hanging in the balance, any amount you’re able to contribute today makes an impact.](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em-dnc-august-2025?amount=3&refcode=em_20250830_4453_fr&refcode2=4453_22930333_v83_cR&akid=4453%2E22930333%2Ev83_cR)
Thank you for your support,
The Democrats

_If you gave recently via check or mail, our system may not have yet processed your gift. We are appreciative and thank you greatly for your support._

Friend, We’re just one month away from the expiration of 2025 government funding. That means, when Congress returns to DC after Labor Day weekend, we’ll be finalizing the 2026 budget to be voted on this fall. **While Republicans have shown that they’re willing to slash everything from healthcare to nutrition in order to hand even more tax breaks to billionaires, we’re demanding a government that puts our communities’ needs first.** [**Click here to add your name and demand Congress invest in our communities with the forthcoming 2026 budget.**](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/u001.qUXRBnqZ7T8nxbpAcIVwoUsWLkUMWlo_0JmZttSlm39Vp4gkIh90ovDrixVFaBLcxHZnxSlQjdtj1uPs2SHrhjwwlhfbe_Ce1rqFYRd17qR57HtsQeueSTphl3f4OLEXj_MEG9g9Qjl2-83Ep9UudgDLLmmUVy4X7ostSciuRGAL5ebkgfr8Lk1YRPpz4nswaLjPob73FaXNwDKvHc5kkpmOMh4KipDCyRlhqZqSugpeP7HoH4XB8XFFZtOlPdiO20KWeTZpavz44CEBvyAZO8x6DS7_qDEu0y9tlfccJZA6cmCHvjnUHILJJ_ORWmzuG8ZU7EHSKYAiftnGXO6gyeU4cbUVBVOo50X40QWzbi-h8h1CKJNn6bun1CI9P5zCco6X96mpMcAkZwOsulGeJFezdntjATxIYPNIbuGVhis5Jf37BoW7Kuhq1046AVkuKIpDH3ruCsDrDTO0bPwmXWPSgnge8r522l1LTRoIUW8/4jh/ZewZlSQhTACEbTrG9NUfkQ/h1/h001.ePhL1oLkE6rjU_yfyoeawYmHYGMPbTt2TcC6nqNBLck) | [ADD YOUR NAME](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/u001.qUXRBnqZ7T8nxbpAcIVwoUsWLkUMWlo_0JmZttSlm39Vp4gkIh90ovDrixVFaBLcxHZnxSlQjdtj1uPs2SHrhjwwlhfbe_Ce1rqFYRd17qR57HtsQeueSTphl3f4OLEXzA9zIelBmyqudkp1wE1GeE3Xd9ot0ew-_siBO3WkMH3qSfqICcRyK51z9Jf9RbHQLMWxfqaQBIpRWlgJGwXwxNKL2-LBy_l9h3e8LBkUuly9xy1o3I85U7F6_CYolwGkZwXyb9Xir1X9RYrGNobjgJElBsUPpkrrdX6rxe5Lzw6tAv9Lc7E3gL1Oms460iLyRQpncyyZNtY_g_St9e9RXcybOs6ogdvtSfMqvAZkY_pkgBwdFst4aszP7_vMzzsdBIHwYGKVu4VgRR7_Zlkatfv5USEO-ecMCPfThHNxqDI5S_MRFNyWvSOftRS_ZQm20UzURVd3BntoDNJPoO8G47Vc6Vx8gShEfES96VH6aL0/4jh/ZewZlSQhTACEbTrG9NUfkQ/h2/h001.xI5MYSaS-YPpFYvs7gt2xCA0zxSYsKUV_eqRGbldi_0)

Here are some of the priorities that are critical to my constituents, and which I’m demanding Congress include in appropriations bills:
**$1.5 billion for Lead Service Line Replacement.** These funds would allow states to identify, remove, and replace all lead service lines within the next decade. We’ve doubled the number of supporting Congresspeople since last year.
**$500 million for the Low Income Household Water Assistance Program.** Funding to help pay off water debts, bills, and other water expenses has expired. It’s urgently needed to keep people connected to safe, clean drinking water.
**$600 million for State Veterans Homes.** This has bipartisan support and would allow critical investments in long-term and supportive care homes for veterans.
[**Add your name to tell Congress to advocate for 2026 Appropriations funding for veterans’ housing and clean drinking water—including lead abatement and support with water payments.**](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/u001.qUXRBnqZ7T8nxbpAcIVwoUsWLkUMWlo_0JmZttSlm39Vp4gkIh90ovDrixVFaBLcxHZnxSlQjdtj1uPs2SHrhjwwlhfbe_Ce1rqFYRd17qR57HtsQeueSTphl3f4OLEXCakBjTDR2IprVMHiAQq2Hf5lDOLhtllCyVKfkEXGLUHcEj5VjCAJO6ZOQf8x2EJ5IwWN5PHOzKZVke_rnT_SbBt6g9I6vtvnnpP7Lnfoqn7FdZ5RP-nDJOv28P9XIfwBhdk2sppthoLNikFIWqf4LCfmYpVtjpVOWH9WIChCiffff7LABZqo1Eyax8YehQewX9fYvTJ6pIgIYB-oWeE8i2HfA-KWQijLMQA-zIatsijeKJlOj_t5SNs5__ImkAbLRLSMp11XyR-fjdknMEiHeiKZE5zsx2Ed2t6b_oMM-CuUNAkb6WD33IkCiy8ISralNGx1trXoAKIr32qwz8qBhmEHswC_mbzjcqmkaoMNGcQ/4jh/ZewZlSQhTACEbTrG9NUfkQ/h3/h001.Dre1O0j-cGoIEjgMVmYGDY9FbYE1sWeYoQ7dhNy8na0)
Other funding priorities I will never stop fighting for include universal healthcare (including healthcare for veterans), expanded childcare benefits, and household food safety. I’ll continue pushing back against Republicans’ dangerous funding cuts that put our families at risk, and I will continue holding the Trump administration accountable for illegally withholding congressionally allocated funds and illegally dismantling federal agencies.
Together, we’re demanding our government put people first, not corporations or billionaires.
With you in this fight,
Rashida

name: Morgan Stahr, United for Democracy
subject: Rigging the data
disclaimer_text: Paid for by United for Democracy
body:
The only way you’ll know the economy is crashing is by looking at your bank account. | |
---
Peter, **Trump doesn’t want you to know he’s failing.** He recently fired Bureau of Labor Statistics commissioner Erika McEntarfer because she published jobs data he didn’t like. **In her place, he’s tapped a Heritage Foundation toadie who thinks we should stop doing a monthly jobs report and spouts trickle-down economic lies that have been debunked for decades.** Small businesses, policymakers, and workers need the monthly jobs numbers to make economic decisions. Instead, Trump wants loyalists to give us rigged quarterly numbers about how great his economy is—even if it’s failing. **The only way you’ll know the economy is crashing is by looking at your bank account.** [**The public needs the truth in order to be informed participants in our democracy. Tell the Senate to stop Trump from firing truth-tellers and installing loyalists in their place. Reject his nominee for Bureau of Labor Statistics now!**](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/u001.qUXRBnqZ7T8nxbpAcIVwoUsWLkUMWlo_0JmZttSlm3_-6ezreVUDKM4uYMpG61B3Zd5nTtvB_529QnSSoQN3UIT5Vm8cymNlg0FkZiV9jch0RG0vYHDYit8WloyIcgoHQN-3gUq5cskhhI48ktokbynjcvPoKa-kOiqzZwpAs15t0gq0FmLXDtNEzdXRavaDW1TpUnmZaRny11GA0c0XLJOTKMmFt5fV870lOOSrt9gclAAP1FN5Y_ujGml50tgvcTtzAKsp27JJZdYV3UHJE93do9-9xYHLDo9VCuBzlhicPiHFIJed5yKFb3LchWDXbkSHx-JtQ_moMwO4TfWhKhEfVfOQX8ikdBKHE2Py0cJ6L9s4xbLxeFORk3vUjLogCMlflb3-ofdU8y7nWnGq_Ov4IsZquKzVacQ8pU7h7J7X150b-zO54ZullvzRKxiVizq0V-gBRWzwp4uNnt6S507F6jQ2EJLiKNv5eT5f0hxlkaATHGgk4Ze8AFoQOIVz/4jh/X1fzDba6QZuPhbrqfcD-LQ/h0/h001.JVJ2kSnVynXXQwrPEIDUFzrk9CSWkZFcPOFKwiYiXyw) [SIGN & SEND](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/u001.qUXRBnqZ7T8nxbpAcIVwoUsWLkUMWlo_0JmZttSlm3_-6ezreVUDKM4uYMpG61B3Zd5nTtvB_529QnSSoQN3UIT5Vm8cymNlg0FkZiV9jch0RG0vYHDYit8WloyIcgoHQN-3gUq5cskhhI48ktokbynjcvPoKa-kOiqzZwpAs15t0gq0FmLXDtNEzdXRavaDW1TpUnmZaRny11GA0c0XLJOTKMmFt5fV870lOOSrt9gclAAP1FN5Y_ujGml50tgvcTtzAKsp27JJZdYV3UHJE93do9-9xYHLDo9VCuBzlhgi4_ho3V366f12P3o8pUWO3Kq8hrLlGI1NNI-ZmmqQGw6z16PRuVxGhshG-4cxKfnBjspfhIEkwkXCjt4092NTUOwWiIeNTyQ2RYBMiAXyoxji6XG5RHqzltRc_1ie0ZDjBtaNCbR7S_Ij5okj6PMEAJw5n6GwfsxjLV02c7uB5Svb1Cc3wg69IM-09tuxFIljstgIMoRYkcLgg25IF_RY/4jh/X1fzDba6QZuPhbrqfcD-LQ/h1/h001.bcmw-OKsnwJQcQkmxAXj-fOvftci0cMZNWzOGyV4mGA) **The Supreme Court has allowed this to happen. They’ve given Trump every kingly power he’s asked for, and greenlit nearly all of his illegal actions.** Now, he’s out to rig our sources of economic information—purging honest officials who are bringing the real data to the American people. Without credible data, the markets will lose confidence. Longer-term interest rates will spike and corporations will ramp up price gouging. **Trump's goal is to make sure you don’t know what’s really happening.** **Trump is attacking democracy by attacking the truth.** He’s deflecting the blame for his failed presidency and turning our institutions into his own personal political tools. [**Send a letter to your members of Congress. Tell them you want to know the truth about America’s economy, not just what Trump wants us to think. Demand they reject Trump’s BLS nominee now!**](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/u001.qUXRBnqZ7T8nxbpAcIVwoUsWLkUMWlo_0JmZttSlm3_-6ezreVUDKM4uYMpG61B3Zd5nTtvB_529QnSSoQN3UIT5Vm8cymNlg0FkZiV9jch0RG0vYHDYit8WloyIcgoHQN-3gUq5cskhhI48ktokbynjcvPoKa-kOiqzZwpAs15t0gq0FmLXDtNEzdXRavaDW1TpUnmZaRny11GA0c0XLJOTKMmFt5fV870lOOSrt9gclAAP1FN5Y_ujGml50tgvcTtzAKsp27JJZdYV3UHJE93do9-9xYHLDo9VCuBzlhjQ4AUYvjwghLO_cw8EpR8RYLHpwjEZGSHkkdscNdYlUz5y_oONNI_MTACgSzLnVL2rUFFfTbiJajKJkl85SmXDa6mhBwCf-vv78A10S9E27HnsS4Faq23W_ayzVerasX29lnb8O4pp8hY26wYReobsJBVPTz2vcOylH0ZhwuVHTUQJDGooDfjzxuKBPQoxWx0Yfv_iXONg5VLVdd-3z1xZ/4jh/X1fzDba6QZuPhbrqfcD-LQ/h2/h001.kN7eYgF10R-6QEXBpCP_T9gl31ZiyuJoLJgYUj4up7c) Thank you for fighting authoritarianism, Morgan Stahr

name: Shawn Harris
subject: I’m a Dirt Road Democrat. Here’s what that means.
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY SHAWN FOR GEORGIA
body:
| | [ ](http://clicksp.shawnharrisforcongress.com/f/a/XOqBxg59Ydk_Tr0JFmc5Yw~~/AAPPABA~/Vj8lBc3Xiy0XVrsRVTa1wkzzarJxechEHMTLugJGoRS5kwKZZ2F-izSwHNRNJ-BqeigbO_NQ8jGy-6WXW_yKMjmYMMCwELJwr7azZvlGq5_8Jbras59sVKe3bcMa9cw13tjtpHYn-3arZf3Cy_Z3SzH_IRDbAUc5_DP73TU9wx2VseQfj1KkuTgp7C7wwGzWtlCtWy6905l_B0QaDfgKWBYIbKLBSQjjk8YSSwU6uG1Ab9rc2CtkWKxlervX1HWlnu8lPgsde848Nb-XEXHLQxy_DK0wjO7ipqKQaOKNobMSW6EcEQQ1_FFJXryDYQuN)
---
### Hi, it's Shawn Harris, the Democrat running to unseat Marjorie Taylor Greene.
When I say I'm a _Dirt Road Democrat,_ I don't mean it as a slogan. I mean it as a way of life.
---
| [ ](http://clicksp.shawnharrisforcongress.com/f/a/XOqBxg59Ydk_Tr0JFmc5Yw~~/AAPPABA~/Vj8lBc3Xiy0XVrsRVTa1wkzzarJxechEHMTLugJGoRS5kwKZZ2F-izSwHNRNJ-BqeigbO_NQ8jGy-6WXW_yKMjmYMMCwELJwr7azZvlGq5_8Jbras59sVKe3bcMa9cw13tjtpHYn-3arZf3Cy_Z3SzH_IRDbAUc5_DP73TU9wx2VseQfj1KkuTgp7C7wwGzWtlCtWy6905l_B0QaDfgKWBYIbKLBSQjjk8YSSwU6uG1Ab9rc2CtkWKxlervX1HWlnu8lPgsde848Nb-XEXHLQxy_DK0wjO7ipqKQaOKNobMSW6EcEQQ1_FFJXryDYQuN)
---
Dirt Road Democrats know what it's like to drive 30 miles to the nearest doctor because our rural hospitals have closed. We know what it's like to send our kids to underfunded schools that struggle to keep teachers. **We know how it feels to be told our communities don 't matter while Washington caters to corporate donors and political extremes.** That's the reality of life in northwest Georgia. **_And it 's why I'm running for Congress against Marjorie Taylor Greene._** Marjorie Taylor Greene has made herself a national celebrity, but what has she done for us? She voted against expanding broadband. She voted against investing in rural infrastructure. She voted against lowering the cost of prescription drugs. Every chance she's had to deliver for our communities, she's chosen partisan grandstanding instead. I'm building a campaign that's about the opposite: organizing in every county, knocking on every door, and making sure rural Georgia is heard. **_Dirt Road Democrats don 't leave anyone behind, and neither will I._** [**But building this kind of campaign takes resources. clean_body: | | Hi, it's Shawn Harris, the Democrat running to unseat Marjorie Taylor Greene. When I say I'm a Dirt Road Democrat, I don't mean it as a slogan. I mean it as a way of life. | Dirt Road Democrats know what it's like to drive 30 miles to the nearest doctor because our rural hospitals have closed. We know what it's like to send our kids to underfunded schools that struggle to keep teachers. We know how it feels to be told our communities don 't matter while Washington caters to corporate donors and political extremes. That's the reality of life in northwest Georgia. And it 's why I'm running for Congress against Marjorie Taylor Greene. Marjorie Taylor Greene has made herself a national celebrity, but what has she done for us? She voted against expanding broadband. She voted against investing in rural infrastructure. She voted against lowering the cost of prescription drugs. Every chance she's had to deliver for our communities, she's chosen partisan grandstanding instead. I'm building a campaign that's about the opposite: organizing in every county, knocking on every door, and making sure rural Georgia is heard. Dirt Road Democrats don 't leave anyone behind, and neither will I. ~~ ~~ ~~ | I'm a fifth-generation farmer and a veteran. I believe in hard work, service, and telling the truth. Marjorie Taylor Greene believes in conspiracy theories, chaos, and cable news clips. Let's give GA-14 the serious, steady leadership it deserves. Thanks for your support, Shawn Harris | ~~ "Facebook" ~ "X" \[BLUESKY\] "Bluesky" PAID FOR BY SHAWN FOR GEORGIA

body:
Join the mass call today at 1pm ET, and find an event near you this weekend. | | | Peter — Across the country, working people will be coming together this weekend to demand a government that puts workers over billionaires. [**Join the Workers Over Billionaires mass call TODAY, Saturday August 30, at 1pm ET**](https://an.workingfamilies.org/ss/c/u001.XaF8mXqsA6b2dSPmhsleMYKt_PNbTZJM77xb-PM80u5dz4nlpjrgMom-6L9Ff_-5TAgz7B92Uq7zxYbLmX8XqA1rd8P5pQ_aOn-c7gE8SK8CfVCsbj2pxzrQJp2AVg8ya0BHcMTvbxdkHhgyDFWy1bpRWl4CFFVx7mbm9PFURT5BO-iursN-lq7An3-QTn9ImREk0PmO-6VjfbSB64KImSxPcmALqwO64n58v6TctzVVJOg8dh_jB1A_EA9MDB76qyJYCYlr_tldAJtRVIKStLHbu5ZxbP3qFnza-kDUyWC6RWBvM6nW9YrPA4scwkl3/4jh/amoDeuuLTqiD6wWz4PRvcw/h0/h001.h3ryZr-WRkD2pkUe9Y122NU0xhbgwbQSY7GI_CVx8L0), then [**RSVP to an event taking place near you this weekend.**](https://an.workingfamilies.org/ss/c/u001.XaF8mXqsA6b2dSPmhsleMYKt_PNbTZJM77xb-PM80u7DE4zve99K6NBNnjKOHUHljgnToN7dlzP18853BTAVpPeS1KIVcsTK2ykIyjvYJuemSKG0bcLH6wrcFzHjczLMYVBlcgyzY0Ln5U51O1xoFPo6NrZIlrh3kqmmiWwummc6n7lPBFIiOpg12xQVeQJbHER8QuFC_okq2HnIPrHHebtNcMK23ZqStPx3ne3hHAmNu9sRLL7UuNoG7l7heO58DUM1W2a-pefTQpvNa1l2uPS8fIADq-HNuObzLyTPdbGPWf6OOsakAKwSnRzduH0Y/4jh/amoDeuuLTqiD6wWz4PRvcw/h1/h001.XVzdAYQg4VUcR8enjfshYQbkoSdjD2NtF2qH-54piTQ) Team WFP \---------- Forwarded message --------- From: Working Families Party
Date: Tuesday, August 26, 2025
Subject: Stand with workers this weekend Peter, We can all see what’s happening in front of us: **Billionaires are trying to take over our entire government.** They want to turn it into their own personal slush fund — firing workers, gutting our healthcare, slashing our social security, raising the cost of living, and then using fear and force to try and silence our voices when we speak out. **But just like any bad boss, billionaires can be stopped when we come together.** That’s why this Labor Day, we won’t just be kicking back with our friends and families, we’ll also be uniting in protests, rallies, and community gatherings across the country. [**Hear from WFP National Director Maurice Mitchell, get front line reports from DC and Chicago, and learn about how working people across the country plan to stand up to the billionaire agenda on a mass call this Saturday, August 30th at 1pm ET/10am Across the country, from big cities to the smallest towns, working people will be coming together to demand a government that puts working people over billionaires. clean_body: Join the mass call today at 1pm ET, and find an event near you this weekend. | | | Peter — Across the country, working people will be coming together this weekend to demand a government that puts workers over billionaires. , then Team WFP \---------- Forwarded message --------- From: Working Families Party Date: Tuesday, August 26, 2025 Subject: Stand with workers this weekend Peter, We can all see what’s happening in front of us: Billionaires are trying to take over our entire government. They want to turn it into their own personal slush fund — firing workers, gutting our healthcare, slashing our social security, raising the cost of living, and then using fear and force to try and silence our voices when we speak out. But just like any bad boss, billionaires can be stopped when we come together. That’s why this Labor Day, we won’t just be kicking back with our friends and families, we’ll also be uniting in protests, rallies, and community gatherings across the country. This Labor Day is about more than just a barbecue. Across the country, from big cities to the smallest towns, working people will be coming together to demand a government that puts working people over billionaires. And this is also about more than one day. It’s about building a movement strong enough to stop the billionaire takeover and create a fairer, more just, and more democratic country for all of us. Stand with workers. Say no to billionaires taking over our country. , then In solidarity, Working Families Party Thoughts? Feedback? We want to hear it. Reply directly to this email and let us know. This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. If you received this email as a forward and want to get the latest updates from the Working Families Party directly, The Working Families Party is the party of the multiracial working class, fighting for a nation that cares for all of us. Wanna learn more? And if you need to update your contact information, Other political groups have corporate donors. But we can pool our resources too. name: Chris Taylor HQ
subject: BREAKING: Justice Rebecca Bradley just announced she’s NOT running for reelection
disclaimer_text: Paid for by The Long Run PAC
body:
Now that the incumbent is bowing out of the race, right-wing special interests will waste no time recruiting and bankrolling another extreme candidate to take on Judge Chris Taylor.
| You may be receiving this email because of your previous support for organizations like The Long Run PAC. We need your support, but if you’d prefer not to receive emails from us, you can [unsubscribe here](https://act.mandelabarnes.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe?t=1&akid=5596%2E196590%2EFN2neo). [](https://act.mandelabarnes.com/go/4612?t=2&akid=5596%2E196590%2EFN2neo)
---
| The Long Run PAC said we could reach out with breaking news, folks:

**Justice Rebecca Bradley, one of the most extreme justices ever to sit on the Wisconsin Supreme Court, just announced she’s NOT running for reelection.**
---
[_**Before we say more, can you pitch in to support our campaign and The Long Run? The uncertainty surrounding our race has increased tenfold, and we need to be prepared for whatever comes next.**_](https://act.mandelabarnes.com/go/4661?t=3&akid=5596%2E196590%2EFN2neo)
| | | [DONATE ](https://act.mandelabarnes.com/go/4662?t=4&akid=5596%2E196590%2EFN2neo)
---
Folks, this news changes everything.

**Now that the incumbent is bowing out of the race, right-wing special interests will waste no time recruiting and bankrolling another extreme candidate to take on Judge Chris Taylor.**

They’ll do whatever it takes to buy back control of our court, and we can’t waste a minute preparing to stop them.

If we’re going to defend reproductive rights, protect workers’ rights, and safeguard our democracy by electing Judge Chris Taylor, we need to be ready before the right-wing money machine kicks into gear.

**That means acting today, folks.** If we wait until the far-right picks their candidate, it will be too late. The only way we can protect our majority is by going on offense today.

[_**Will you chip in $10 or whatever you can to support Judge Chris Taylor and the Long Run and help us fight back?**_](https://act.mandelabarnes.com/go/4661?t=6&akid=5596%2E196590%2EFN2neo)

Thank you for taking action,

Team Taylor
_Judge Chris Taylor is a judge on the Wisconsin Court of Appeals and a candidate for Wisconsin Supreme Court in 2026._

subject: Expanding the Senate map in 2026

body:
It’s the only way we’ll be able to take back our Democratic majority
#
**Georgia. Michigan. Minnesota. New Hampshire.**
In 2026, defending these critical U.S. Senate seats won’t be enough.
**We need to expand the map.**
We need to flip **Maine** and **North Carolina.**
And we desperately need to start organizing voters in states like **Ohio** and **Iowa** and **Florida** and **Texas.**
**That’s the only way we can take back the U.S. Senate in 2026.**
**Will you help us get it done by chipping in $5 or more today?**

name: Jane Fonda via Leaders We Deserve
subject: Tyranny in D.C. got my blood boiling
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY LEADERS WE DESERVE, LEADERSWEDESERVE
body:
Peter,

Hi, it’s Jane Fonda. Leaders We Deserve invited me to send this message to
you, because they know how urgently we need to act.

Donald Trump is threatening to send troops into Chicago — and Illinois
Governor JB Pritzker is giving him the only answer he deserves: "Do not
come to Chicago."

The Progressive Change Campaign Committee (PCCC) is blasting this message
into Chicago streets on a rolling digital billboard truck alongside
another truth: "FASCISTS ARE NOT WELCOME."

This truck will circle Trump Tower and drive through Chicago’s busiest
streets — with information helping people know their rights and learn how
to fight back when Trump’s troopers invade.

When I saw this, I donated right away. If you are able, I hope you will
too. [ https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em_ms_lwd_20250830_1_pccc?refcode=em20250830-2597&amount=5&refcode2=2597_587540_QqPn6n&akid=2597%2E587540%2EQqPn6n ]Can you chip in $5 to help the PCCC keep this truck
rolling in Chicago — and to support Leaders We Deserve’s continued fight
against Trump’s oppressive agenda?

[ https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em_ms_lwd_20250830_1_pccc?amount=5&refcode=em20250830-2597&refcode2=2597_587540_QqPn6n&akid=2597%2E587540%2EQqPn6n ]Donate $5 »

What Trump is doing in Washington, D.C. is not just about intimidation —
it’s about rehearsal.

Every checkpoint, every armored vehicle, every overwhelming show of force
is an exercise in how to control and subdue American cities. They used
what they learned in Los Angeles when they descended on D.C., and they’ll
use what they’ve learned in D.C. if they come to Chicago. And it won't
stop there. We need to ensure people know their rights and know how to
fight back.

I’ve spent decades fighting for peace, justice, democracy, and climate
action. At this stage of my life, I refuse to stand by while our cities
are being occupied — with people living under the weight of armed troops
and overwhelming, oppressive federal force. Silence in moments like this
is not an option.

[ https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em_ms_lwd_20250830_1_pccc?refcode=em20250830-2597&amount=5&refcode2=2597_587540_QqPn6n&akid=2597%2E587540%2EQqPn6n ]Can you chip in whatever you can so the PCCC can make sure Governor
Pritzker’s defiance — and Chicago’s resistance — is shouted from every
corner of the city, and help fuel Leaders We Deserve’s critical work to
confront Trump’s abuses?

History is being written in real time. Together, let’s make sure it
records that people rose up — loudly, visibly, and with determination — to
confront and stop Trump’s authoritarian power grab.

Thank you for standing with me,

— Jane Fonda

[ https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em_ms_lwd_20250830_1_pccc?refcode=em20250830-2597&amount=5&refcode2=2597_587540_QqPn6n&akid=2597%2E587540%2EQqPn6n ]Jane Fonda

This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is a critical way we communicate with grassroots supporters like you. However, if you would like to change your subscription preferences, here are some options:

Receive Fewer Emails: https://act.leaderswedeserve.com/signup/receive-fewer-emails/?t=1001&akid=2597%2E587540%2EQqPn6n
Update Your Information: https://act.leaderswedeserve.com/signup/update-your-information/
Unsubscribe: https://act.leaderswedeserve.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?t=1002&akid=2597%2E587540%2EQqPn6n

Leaders We Deserve
611 Pennsylvania Ave SE
#192
Washington, DC 20003
info@leaderswedeserve.com

[PAID FOR BY LEADERS WE DESERVE, LEADERSWEDESERVE.COM, NOT AUTHORIZED BY ANY CANDIDATE OR CANDIDATE'S COMMITTEE]

Everyone is focused on our campaign for Governor here in Michigan. Governors are quickly becoming the first and last line of defense against the bully in the White House. And in our battleground state, the fight for our future couldn’t be more important.
Right now, we're leading in the polls, and momentum is on our side. But we can’t rest. **We need your help to ensure this campaign has the grassroots support to win in a state as competitive as Michigan.**
I’m running against well funded opponents who are lagging in the polls but pulling out all the stops to get ahead. The anti-public education DeVos family has already picked their candidate on the Republican side with a $5 million check, and GOP donors are backing an independent candidate in the race trying to spoil the ability for Democrats to win. We need to show everyone that our campaign is ready for whatever they throw at us, and **that we’re here to win.**
[**Can I count on you to make a donation today? disclaimer_text: Paid for by Miyares for Virginia |
body:
Friend, this could happen to your son.| | | Friend, This is outrageous.

**Two teenage boys in Loudoun County were forced to share a locker room with a biological girl who was filming them. **

When they spoke out about this violation, **LCPS suspended them,** then accused **THEM** of sexual harassment.
---
[](http://links.jasonforvirginia.com/s/c/86LEYngjk_MubF8q6_CXeM_Ushf6i9EtDYSnhNNb-JpE-MwU9kJeQ6WvbwH49yjXDv6Kw7v_2Xvh3rGLMXEaonC8dUE9dWGJrXgCk6dvowG5Hp1W_kOnpK94up6I07DEN8PBWo2RLVgwHFH50vYBY6-Pu0kCOYYdv4xUHAUcKym5XgemzS_278Odd0ZQRCYQhznI0Xs-qs98b1aa5mm6HJD0tvCxhCW7IsuDTOAi10oJpqGTqFqyoVsGVkAocXfIMGSYBMAUXWm1l7youdC95Fm7Fi2_RBLzrUzJIF5LmssL-k3VXGj0x4on99hP_5LfbwAgF6vyP8SnqrWdT3c3shxBkDmbkyfIFnbGlC1ul5ekmQYqvNFO4V3JGUEv9XaAj36Q7uRpj1EqYVuXonfATzG4LwSQfH3kpG_6UgRlxa-mb2iL7B8nhe6VKYVM0IGQUp2_DWNTIhz4Ol_CXdky-YUUGA45OFsi8FIDsbc7oaxCCnA9oySVQYNxeDoAIHWG6z2z4hr4j9VIDv1V_KIqOCqdGyBUMhDON15mvyaoOBfgtdm0-pdisGhFkr_TCRoMexpfhukUfWryiyweIg24/TaDaWD473mKRmRBhyz2vZw5399j7IPKI/12)
---
The victims became the accused.

That's when Governor Youngkin directed me to investigate LCPS. **We found them in violation of Title IX** — breaking federal law to protect their radical agenda instead of protecting our kids.
---
[ **STAND FOR COMMON SENSE** ](http://links.jasonforvirginia.com/s/c/emOagdkIdtLM48Q35BXHPJekCV3XvcP2I48fflHNu4TeQQaEirS9E5MCD9paGWq3J5es22fJbiJCH92p8C-eWYET5e9QXpaBHMJfgJr57_7sF7bLqPE0ujuaeXseDIP91kpuvh06989d_tWlhxOEysIW5WEz7fGnLNdVd3HciwWtyrOYR0E1CFRAghBg4SXF-iNDNhZAFsLKsJtaMSGvjbmAc4wE6CXuFzjNmUqeBUL-QQu7pqSpWPBpLozykNeSKPrb4VuJI2I4X3Q8p98S0qMEM-OV6rGQxVV1K37NBYfqud_M2_CgZI8Tlakg1XanfRMv41K3OwdpsP8IkkGm4g4b3b4vubOdhlkuSdT-k9vl23ENKH_b62AASjHtXsfXdMQkw681yPDgUGy-ATLMw1Mc12NeIfPMv35ayzDRAW3-jyjycXp5RIfxrgLMyr2BFZSp1IAoASnPj0ef-CSXz9-LiEIuPlds8I7Zo6DSFSb5bpGlk-yvpxHeJQPfcRi_NkjElBsAf1oTXhd0rjbk0OLaP-wYdKJkFDYWKuhHZS2ODXrr2EArZZfNX3o6fHuihnX_PqXGaV8WGN4GSseI/-PfSpggDZvyixOYwNC1W9Jem9Z3tJAFJ/12)
---
**Jay Jones cheers on these same policies.** He wants this madness in every Virginia school, putting more children at risk.

**Our children deserve protection, not persecution.**

I've spent my career defending victims and prosecuting those who harm our kids. That's exactly what I'm doing here — standing up for students when their own schools fail them.

**No one should be punished for believing what’s taught in biology class.**

👉 [_**Chip in now to help me keep fighting for our children's safety and dignity.**_](http://links.jasonforvirginia.com/s/c/NGzWrTLHBFELPNea-1XXYhDJpJ6rC-LXJM0QoG_Mavcm0JUo_pAUISLITnW4VGOvx-0vnprTMDwJZwGxV_AdSajxF6FHlvoKUjeCWQdfk_TVKgojC6aQIKYl0VIqneAPoN14fMJvuQsHG_JfezuNUhTuuIokRaqc2U2oiq704WoBMfxjeI4YeY75CpSh7AbcKmA64iLr81_YNMVCda8UFbCC4Oe8PZx4nzmc-H8Dr2A8Bv1JK1C0TgYdaQWMNJ3wL6muDdycYCBDOQlXazOzGw9kGFnifPo6YMjcdhnjJb48bpF8nQg3KVBvPdGgo9b7GOPJCi0mnafPhY0JGinXC-d8It5AyMeD9QSHPGcnjtMYozaqH4uWTyZWd9ZbbeUjILLLS4SbloBj0gfDJWVEMRxyl-rSCY7BUMJgv0jnb2uWiCZQkzjI1qTUGC0e49UdV2gy3mgiTcIZ9oL_7xKJ4zjl9qrXJOb5vfX8_xzhVnF4irUs_KdCfByHN9-Ts0s_yD02a98ChtHlPJHik_B2w4SDR18TRefTdK7Uclf7APlMKPGJLtqlgxor-skH8vCQioCV7o9XBW3Wh4tP242L/7SuCeGlb0DdLsCNZKnlcT4PgAznjKANe/12)

Attorney General Jason Miyares

name: The Spanberger Campaign
subject: This has to be our top priority
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Spanberger for Governor
body:
It's critical that we launch our ad in new markets throughout the Commonwealth.

[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/114304567/566587739/603473497?refcode=BLU_EM_ASFG_2025-08-30_1&amount=25&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjQvMS8xMDM4NTgiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiNmMyMmM5MjUtYjQ4NS1mMDExLWI0ODQtNjA0NWJkZWI3NDEzIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=sC92LiZN-4osioBR_cGLGUStGUOj_yMyO652YCGUNWY=)
Peter, let's be clear: Trump's new law raises prices on Virginians.
But our opponent, Winsome Earle-Sears, has bragged about it. She says a law that raises healthcare, housing, and utility costs "does so many great things."
_It's time for Virginians to know that Sears puts Trump before Virginia._
**That's why we just launched a TV and digital ad to hold her accountable.**
And it's critical that we launch this ad in new markets throughout the Commonwealth.

clean_body: It's critical that we launch our ad in new markets throughout the Commonwealth. = Peter, let's be clear: Trump's new law raises prices on Virginians. But our opponent, Winsome Earle-Sears, has bragged about it. She says a law that raises healthcare, housing, and utility costs "does so many great things." It's time for Virginians to know that Sears puts Trump before Virginia. That's why we just launched a TV and digital ad to hold her accountable. Friend,
---
Believe me, writing this letter was not my first choice...
---
_As a matter of fact, it’s my _last_ _resort_. _
---
**You see, I’m writing to you as a man who’s run out of time, resources, and options ahead of the TRIAL OF MY LIFE.**
---
**A Democrat Attorney General is making me stand in a rigged trial for bogus state-level crimes, because she knows that not even the most powerful man on the face of the planet – President Trump – can save me with a _federal_ pardon in _state_ court.**
---
And so, today, I’m not reaching out as America’s Mayor, not as a former prosecutor, not even as President Trump’s former attorney, but as a grandfather and a patriot who’s staring down a 20-year prison sentence for doing what I believed – _and KNOW_ – was RIGHT.
---
The Democrats and their Deep State goons think they’ve finally got me. They’ve left me with no escape, no lifeline – **especially after they’ve already STRIPPED me of my law licenses!**
---
_No viable pardon from the President.
No protection.
No cavalry coming over the hill.
_
---
But even though the most powerful man in the country legally cannot step in to save me, the most powerful _force_ absolutely can: _THE AMERICAN PEOPLE_.
---
So, with my back against the wall, I’m turning to the one thing that can save me:**_the collective voices of MILLIONS of patriots like _YOU_._**
---

Thank you and God bless you, Rudy Giuliani Rudy Giuliani Freedom Fund Paid for by Rudy Giuliani Freedom Fund Legal Defense Trust

body:
#
Peter, we don’t like to use scumbag scare tactics to fundraise. It’s not how we do things here at VoteVets.
We shoot straight, tell you what we’re doing, tell you how we’ll use your donation, and hope that works.
But we’ve got to share something a little worrying, because we’re spending a lot of time trying to figure out how to counter it. Take a look at this headline:
[](https://act.votevets.org/go/2927?c0=vv-pac-em-aug25.fgigcd&refcode=em250830-1-120&t=1&akid=34579%2E3013755%2EvgeJ09)
A lot of factors go into winning campaigns, but one of the most important factors is which candidate is able to raise enough money to bring voters to their side and turn them out to the polls.
Right now, the GOP is winning that fight.
VoteVets has spent a lot of time and resources recruiting and supporting the candidates we’ll need to win back the House in 2026. We’ve got an impressive slate, and we have more than enough to win back a majority in that chamber — if they’re successful. But if MAGA candidates drown out their messages in attack ads they’re at serious risk of losing.
That’s where VoteVets comes in. We run the hardest hitting ads in politics. Ads that speak directly to voters that often get overlooked. Ads that swing races and win majorities. When our candidates are getting pounded by MAGA attacks, we fight back.
That’s why we kickstarted the VoteVets Patriot Fund earlier this summer. We’re going to use whatever we raise into that fund to win back Congress next year, and we need your help to fill our warchest.
VoteVets is one of the few organizations that can turn the tide, but we can’t do it without you. So, please:

We’ll route it directly to the Patriot Fund, and we’ll make sure it goes towards defeating MAGA Republicans in the must-win seats we need to take back our majority in 2026 and put much needed checks on Trump’s authoritarian White House.**](https://act.votevets.org/go/2927?c0=vv-pac-em-aug25.fgigcd&refcode=em250830-1-120&t=2&akid=34579%2E3013755%2EvgeJ09)
