name: Party Registration HQ
subject: Why would you ignore this, Friend?
body:
Please see a special message from our sponsor:

Unsubscribe
http://trk.victorymailings.com/?xul=2qq8ny363wdaxf3eu1ztoj206xq2ssg868dnjp2ft5unn1a84u1rdzj04bnfdm159pflhwbkd4fm40d7n422grgsxft401pyebge733tc&__stmp=t1tcee&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh

Please see a special message from our sponsor:

http://trk.victorymailings.com/?&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=t1tcee&__onlt=t

Dear Fellow American,

I am enclosing a survey on a very urgent matter.

It will only take a moment of your time, but the topic is vital to our nation's future.

A movement is growing, led by progressives—but supported by many well-meaning Americans—to change the way we elect our president. In effect, it seeks to do away with the Electoral College as devised by the Framers of our Constitution.

That's why Hillsdale College is conducting a National Survey on Presidential Selection.

By completing this survey and returning it to me as soon as possible, you will help Hillsdale College more clearly understand the views of mainstream Americans concerning this issue—views we will make available to policymakers and opinion leaders.

You can complete the National Survey on Presidential Selection by using the link below:

http://trk.victorymailings.com/?xtl=rc6qmltxol1jj0nhhzbuu22meyr441gaxzigntd2b9qhrg6w4bljev6bgcaknqsrti4rv7asiqj2huy0475edqzv3pbu5apar4wbbtloj4r1omlpc66yo6siiqkgcmhkhijhe7row3t1vw2b6vji&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=t1tcee&__onlt=t
http://trk.victorymailings.com/?&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=t1tcee&__onlt=t

Do you think that most Americans understand how the Electoral College works and its role in preserving free and representative government?

YES
http://trk.victorymailings.com/?&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=t1tcee&__onlt=t

NO
http://trk.victorymailings.com/?&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=t1tcee&__onlt=t

For most of our nation's history, the Electoral College, as devised by the Framers of our Constitution, was not controversial. Indeed, it was considered essential to preserving free and representative government.

But today, more than a dozen states have joined together in an attempt to undermine this constitutional bulwark of liberty.

This movement to undermine the Constitution has grown largely because of the failure of America's schools to provide young people with a proper grounding in American civics—too many Americans simply don't understand the importance of the Constitution, including the Electoral College, to liberty.

Because of this, Hillsdale College has expanded its educational mission on behalf of liberty nationwide, directly teaching millions of Americans in all 50 states.

These efforts, which aim to counter the Left's domination over most of our schools, include:

Teaching millions of citizens nationwide through free online courses, such as "Constitution 101" and "The Great American Story: A Land of Hope;"

Reaching over six million households and businesses nationwide with Imprimis, Hillsdale's free digest of liberty;

Promoting constitutional education through social media outlets popular with young people;

Sending millions of pocket-size copies of America's founding documents to citizens and K-12 schools across America; and

Restoring excellence to K-12 education by helping establish and guiding classical K-12 schools nationwide and providing, free of charge, Hillsdale's American classical K-12 curriculum (including American civics) to thousands of American educators and homeschoolers.

Your answers to the National Survey on Presidential Selection will help Hillsdale form the most effective strategy to educate millions of Americans about the importance of the Constitution to liberty—including the importance of the Electoral College.

And this is especially important in this critical time for America's future.

So please share your views on this urgent topic by completing our National Survey on Presidential Selection and returning it to me as soon as possible. It will only take a few minutes of your time.

You can take the survey using this secure link I've provided below:

http://trk.victorymailings.com/?xtl=rc6qmltxol1jj0nhhzbuu22meyr441gaxzigntd2b9qhrg6w4bljev6bgcaknqsrti4rv7asiqj2huy0475edqzv3pbu5apar4wbbtloj4r1omlpc66yo6siiqkgcmhkhijhe7row3t1vw2b6vji&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=t1tcee&__onlt=t
http://trk.victorymailings.com/?&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=t1tcee&__onlt=t

And after you take your survey, I hope you will also consider supporting Hillsdale College with a tax-deductible gift.

Since 1844, Hillsdale has been "pursuing truth and defending liberty." Today, it does so through its extensive educational outreach efforts while refusing to accept ONE PENNY of government funding—not even indirectly in the form of federal or state student grants or loans.

The support of private citizens like you is essential to our work.

Please represent your state and take the National Survey on Presidential Selection today. Thank you for your time and attention—and for your friendship and support of liberty.

Warm regards,

Larry P. Arnn

President, Hillsdale College

Pursuing Truth and Defending Liberty Since 1844

subject: This is your moment to act
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Powered by People, [poweredxpeople
body:
#
Tomorrow at midnight is our end-of-month fundraising deadline, and we’re still short of our **$15,000 goal.**
This goal isn’t just about keeping the lights on. It’s about making sure we can show up—in every corner of Texas—to empower voters, support volunteers, and keep building the movement our democracy needs.
This summer, we’ve been traveling across Texas and beyond—holding town halls, registering voters, and having real conversations that help rebuild trust in our communities. And it’s working. We’re seeing packed rooms, new volunteers stepping up, and growing momentum on the ground.
**But we’re also up against tough realities: if we can’t close this gap before tomorrow night, we’ll have to scale back.** That means fewer events, fewer resources, and fewer voters reached. That’s not a compromise we want to make.
Powered by People exists to do the hard, everyday work of organizing year-round—especially where others don’t. But we can’t do it without you.
[**Will you chip in to help meet our $15,000 goal ahead of tomorrow’s end-of-month deadline? If you believe in a democracy where people drive change, this is your moment to act.**](https://act.betoorourke.com/go/65953?t=2&akid=240335%2E444211%2EbdVBzg)
**[DONATE](https://act.betoorourke.com/go/65953?t=3&akid=240335%2E444211%2EbdVBzg)**
In solidarity,
Powered by People

| [](https://substack.com/redirect/e8613f86-f224-4666-b4a1-0e8c2ded48ab?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
Photo by [Parsa](https://substack.com/redirect/33819a2a-9bdd-4ec4-aec2-7a985b874da2?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) on [Unsplash](https://substack.com/redirect/a0e4cad2-babd-4d39-8fba-747d42678f50?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
Car tags are expensive in Mississippi and Tippah County leads with the highest taxes in North Mississippi. The median household income in Tippah County is $51,141/year compared to $82,980 in Desoto County. But citizens of Tippah County pay almost 25% more for a car tag.
Your car tag cost is mostly driven by your county's total millage. Same vehicle, same MSRP, same model year -> higher millage = higher tag. Mississippi calculates the tax (ad valorem) on a car as **30% of MSRP** (then reduced each year by a state depreciation schedule) multiplied by your local millage; there's a **statewide "legislative tag credit"** that lowers the bill some, but it's applied uniformly across the state so it doesn't change the county-to-county ranking. Base state fees (registration/privilege) are small and don't vary by county.
# County-to-County comparison
Below is an apples-to-apples snapshot using current **county** millage totals. If you live **inside a city** , expect municipal mills (tax rate) to add on top (e.g., in DeSoto: Hernando ~40.76, Southaven ~46.78, Olive Branch ~38.50, Horn Lake ~50.00).
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/8d0b1e92-9526-4419-80d3-28259c29d7f3?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
Example math (before the statewide tag credit and standard state fees): assessed value = 30% of MSRP = 0.30 × $35,000 = $10,500; gross tax (ad valorem) = $10,500 × (millage/1000).
## What this means in plain English
* **Tippah County is one of the higher taxed counties in the region** , so for the same car you'll typically pay **25 -33% more** than in **DeSoto** or **Tishomingo**. Benton is the closest to Tippah (only 2% lower).
* **Inside-city residents** in _any_ county will see more taxes (city + school + county). Ripley, for example, adds 14.27 mills.
* DeSoto recently **cut county rates** to blunt reassessment increases; that move also tends to **reduce tag costs**.

## Don't forget the small stuff (same statewide)
Everyone pays the same _state_ fees on top (or alongside) the ad valorem tax: **$14 registration for a new tag** (or **$12.75 renewal decal**), plus a small **privilege license** fee (e.g., ~$15 for a car), title fee for new purchases, etc.
Those don't make tags expensive--your local politicians do.
**Read all of Dana 's post and stay informed about politics in Mississippi**

clean_body: Why are taxes so high? Ask your county supervisor! Tippah County is one of the higher taxed counties in the region. | | ---|---|--- | | | Forwarded this email? ? for more ? from the desk ofDana Criswell Why are taxes so high? Ask your county supervisor! | | | Aug 30 | | | | | | | | | | | | | | ---|---|--- Photo by on Car tags are expensive in Mississippi and Tippah County leads with the highest taxes in North Mississippi. The median household income in Tippah County is $51,141/year compared to $82,980 in Desoto County. But citizens of Tippah County pay almost 25% more for a car tag. Your car tag cost is mostly driven by your county's total millage. Same vehicle, same MSRP, same model year -> higher millage = higher tag. Mississippi calculates the tax (ad valorem) on a car as 30% of MSRP (then reduced each year by a state depreciation schedule) multiplied by your local millage; there's a statewide "legislative tag credit" that lowers the bill some, but it's applied uniformly across the state so it doesn't change the county-to-county ranking. Base state fees (registration/privilege) are small and don't vary by county. County-to-County comparison Below is an apples-to-apples snapshot using current county millage totals. If you live inside a city , expect municipal mills (tax rate) to add on top (e.g., in DeSoto: Hernando ~40.76, Southaven ~46.78, Olive Branch ~38.50, Horn Lake ~50.00). | | ---|---|--- Example math (before the statewide tag credit and standard state fees): assessed value = 30% of MSRP = 0.30 × $35,000 = $10,500; gross tax (ad valorem) = $10,500 × (millage/1000). What this means in plain English Tippah County is one of the higher taxed counties in the region , so for the same car you'll typically pay 25 -33% more than in DeSoto or Tishomingo. Benton is the closest to Tippah (only 2% lower). Inside-city residents in any county will see more taxes (city + school + county). Ripley, for example, adds 14.27 mills. DeSoto recently cut county rates to blunt reassessment increases; that move also tends to reduce tag costs. Don't forget the small stuff (same statewide) Everyone pays the same state fees on top (or alongside) the ad valorem tax: $14 registration for a new tag (or $12.75 renewal decal), plus a small privilege license fee (e.g., ~$15 for a car), title fee for new purchases, etc. Those don't make tags expensive--your local politicians do. Read all of Dana 's post and stay informed about politics in Mississippi** | | | | | | | (C) 2025 Dana Criswell Mississippi ? ?

name: News Team @ Our Revolution
subject: UPDATES: The People vs. The Police State, Trump’s Tyranny, Mamdani’s Momentum, and more!
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Our Revolution click here to unsubscribe
body:
[ stdin# ][IMG]

NEWS FROM THE POLITICAL REVOLUTION

HEADLINES: Taking on Trump’s Tyranny | Big Tech = Big Brother | The
People vs. The Police State | Grounding ICE Flights | Victory! DNC to
Curb Dark Money | Mamdani Momentum | New Endorsements | & MORE!

★ ★ ★

Tyranny is rising in America. Trump’s taking over our cities,
silencing dissent, and targeting his "enemies"- including immigrants,
workers, and activists.

“Trump’s federal takeover is more than an abuse of power — it’s a
deliberate merger of authoritarianism and oligarchy,” Our Revolution
executive director Joseph Geevarghese wrote in an op-ed in [ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/33954?t=1011&akid=9295%2E5885482%2EVrteBy ]The
Hill.

Big Tech funded Trump’s return to power, and now his administration
is funneling billions of our tax dollars to build a Big Brother
state, put troops in our streets, and fund cruel detention centers
and deportation flights.

It can all feel dystopian. But Our Revolution REFUSES to sit on the
sidelines. We’re Fighting Oligarchy by exposing the corruption,
unleashing pressure campaigns in the halls of Congress, and deploying
our volunteer-led Citizen Action Teams across the country.

Our activists have protested Crypto Oligarchs at the Trump Golf
Course and led nationwide actions against Trump's Police State
Oligarchs like Palantir and Avelo Airlines. And, we just passed a
resolution to start getting Big Money - like AIPAC and CRYPTO - out
of the Democratic presidential primary in 2028.

We don’t have time to waste. That’s why Our Revolution is focused on
building grassroots power to Fight Trump & the Oligarchs with our
targeted 3 Point Strategy:

1. Fight Trump’s Oligarch Agenda - from Capitol Hill to City Halls
2. Elect Anti-Oligarch Champions up and down the ballot
3. Get Oligarch Money out of Politics and uplift the grassroots

[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/33955?t=1012&akid=9295%2E5885482%2EVrteBy ]And we’re just getting started. Chip in a donation of any amount
to help fuel this people-powered resistance.

★ ★ ★

[3]Fight Trump & The Oligarchs

★ ★ ★

THE PEOPLE VS BIG BROTHER

With nationwide protests from DC to NYC to Seattle, Our Revolution is
sounding the alarm: Big Tech is cashing in on Trump’s surveillance
state — using our tax dollars to spy on us all.

[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/33956?t=1013&akid=9295%2E5885482%2EVrteBy ]Watch our VIRAL video from NYC Palantir Protest!

“These tech giants poured millions into Trump’s campaign. Now they
are reaping billion-dollar federal contracts in return,” Our
Revolution’s Joseph Geevarghese wrote in [ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/33954?t=1014&akid=9295%2E5885482%2EVrteBy ]The Hill. “This is the
modern security state: Big Tech serving the political ambitions of an
authoritarian president.”

At the center is Palantir, billionaire Peter Thiel’s Big Brother
machine. Palantir powers ICE raids, predictive policing, and even
military targeting — all while raking in billions in federal
contracts.

We refuse to bankroll Trump’s tech cronies to spy on our
communities—and we’re fighting back. Add your name to tell Congress
to halt federal funding for Palantir and put people’s privacy above
corporate profits.

[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/33957?t=1015&akid=9295%2E5885482%2EVrteBy ]SIGN THE PETITION

★ ★ ★

Avelo Airlines took a $150M contract to run ICE deportation flights —
turning our airports into launchpads for tearing families apart.

Public outcry followed — protests, billboards, and pressure campaigns
— and it’s working. Our Revolution San Fernando Valley activists
helped ground Avelo’s flights in Burbank, California and up the West
Coast.

Now our activists in Maryland are turning up the heat. At
Baltimore-Washington International (BWI), a state-owned airport, Gov.
Wes Moore and state officials have the power to block Avelo and stand
with immigrant families or let our tax dollars bankroll Trump’s
deportation machine.

If you're near Hanover, MD, [ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/33958?t=1016&akid=9295%2E5885482%2EVrteBy ]RSVP here to join us this Sunday to
demand Gov. Wes Moore cut BWI’s ties with Trump’s deportation
machine. Or [ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/33959?t=1017&akid=9295%2E5885482%2EVrteBy ]click here to sign the petition if you live in
Maryland!

★ ★ ★

[9]Take Back The Party

★ ★ ★

MAINE ACT: BERNIE & TROY!

This Labor Day weekend, Bernie is making a special stop in Portland,
Maine to rally alongside Our Revolution-endorsed [ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/33960?t=1018&akid=9295%2E5885482%2EVrteBy ]Troy Jackson —
logger, union leader, and working-class champion for Governor — and
Senate candidate Graham Platner — oyster farmer, Marine veteran, and
bold challenger to Susan Collins.

It’s time to start scaling up for 2026, fight back against the
billionaire class, and win real power for working people.

★ ★ ★

MAMDANI MOMENTUM

Wow. Andrew Cuomo just told donors at a Hamptons fundraiser that he’s
ready to work with Trump to try to beat Zohran Mamdani in the NYC
mayor’s race.

The establishment is terrified of Mamdani because he is fighting for
policies that serve US — not the donor class. Even Democratic leaders
like Hakeem Jeffries are refusing to unite behind him as the
Democratic nominee.

In the primary, Our Revolution organizers made over 60,000 calls and
texts for Zohran. Our volunteers hit all 5 boroughs — joined by
former US Rep. Jamaal Bowman to canvass voters in subways and key
battleground districts.

Now, we’ve got just a few short weeks to mobilize the troops and go
even BIGGER. We’ll have boots on the ground across the City, but
we’re also firing up our phone bank operation.

[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/33961?t=1019&akid=9295%2E5885482%2EVrteBy ]MAKE CALLS FOR MAMDANI

★ ★ ★

And, Mamdani is not alone. Our Revolution is amassing a wave of
progressive champions for the 2026 Midterms — and we’re ramping up
NOW to out-organize Big Money.

At our recent [ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/33228?t=1020&akid=9295%2E5885482%2EVrteBy ]Fight Oligarchy Town Hall, more than 50,000
activists came together to strategize with congressional candidates,
Donavan McKinney (MI-13) and Robert Peters (IL-02), and Troy Jackson
for Maine Governor.

We’ll be rolling out lots MORE endorsements — from Congress to City
Council — including working class champions like Drew Duncan:

Our Revolution is proud to support Drew Duncan for Kalamazoo City
Commission.

Drew doesn’t just talk progressive change — he’s delivering it. As a
local Our Revolution member, organizer, and community leader, Drew
has been at the forefront of the fight for affordable housing, green
energy, and investment in underserved communities.

This is what Our Revolution was built for: electing bold progressives
at every level of government who will challenge the status quo and
put people over profit. Let’s show what grassroots power can do in
Kalamazoo.

[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/31227?t=1021&akid=9295%2E5885482%2EVrteBy ]Help us ramp up our grassroots GOTV machine to elect progressives
up and down the ballot this November and hit the ground running for
2026 — every dollar helps!

★ ★ ★

[14]Elect Candidates

★ ★ ★

VICTORY! We just won a major milestone in the fight to get Big Money
out of politics. The DNC voted Wednesday to finally crack down on
dark money in presidential primaries.

“Blocking corporate money in the nominating process and blocking dark
money — those are two great steps,” Our Rev Chair Larry Cohen told
[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/33963?t=1022&akid=9295%2E5885482%2EVrteBy ]Common Dreams.

“New progressive candidates have been blocked by the onslaught of
money from crypto, fossil fuel and an endless parade of corporate
spending, as well as AIPAC and other right-wing groups and Republican
billionaires,” Our Revolution Chair Larry Cohen wrote in an op-ed in

[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/33964?t=1023&akid=9295%2E5885482%2EVrteBy ]The Nation.

“This resolution is about putting power back where it belongs — in
the hands of voters, not corporate elites,” said Our Revolution
leader Joseph Geevarghese. “This victory belongs to the grassroots.”

This comes after 10 years organizing, electing progressives into the
party structure, and keeping up grassroots pressure — [ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/33965?t=1024&akid=9295%2E5885482%2EVrteBy ]we
delivered 13,000+ petitions just this week to the DNC.

Now the hard work begins: making sure these reforms are enforced for
2028 and beyond. For too long, Super PACs and billionaires have
drowned out grassroots voices.

Our Revolution will keep leading the charge to get AIPAC, crypto, and
other dark money out of the party — and we won’t stop until primaries
belong to the people. [ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/31227?t=1025&akid=9295%2E5885482%2EVrteBy ]Donate $5, $10, or $27 to fuel the next
phase of this fight.

★ ★ ★

Going into the 2026 mid-terms and the 2028 presidential race, we need
to make sure candidates embrace a progressive agenda that connects
with voters and will deliver for poor and working people in office.

“Harris lost because she did not embrace an economic populist
message. The electorate is angry about their standard of living
declining and about the elites controlling D.C. and enriching
themselves,” Our Revolution’s Joseph Geevarghese told the [ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/33966?t=1026&akid=9295%2E5885482%2EVrteBy ]LA
Times.

To that end, we’re asking Our Revolution supporters like you to help
us shape our candidate endorsement process. Take our [ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/33656?t=1027&akid=9295%2E5885482%2EVrteBy ]Candidate
Priorities Survey to weigh in on what YOU want to see them run on to
WIN in the Midterms.

★ ★ ★

[21]Action Center

★ ★ ★

New Yorkers: This is not the time to sit idle. Hop on a bus Sunday,
August 31st and join the Day of Resistance in the Hamptons as we
protest Oligarchs at the beach!

We’re bringing the fight to the MAGA 1%. [ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/33968?t=1028&akid=9295%2E5885482%2EVrteBy ]Join us here.

Our Mission: Fight Trump and the Oligarchy. We have built a powerful
network of organizers and activists — 60,000+ connected across the
country — and we’re getting to work!

[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/28759?t=1029&akid=9295%2E5885482%2EVrteBy ]Use this sign-up tool to plug in — and pick your top areas of
interest. Whether it’s pressuring lawmakers in their office or
amplifying the message online, there are tons of ways to help!

★ ★ ★

[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/33969?t=1030&akid=9295%2E5885482%2EVrteBy ]RSVP now for our next National Organize to Win Call on Monday,
September 15th! We’ll be discussing our work to fight Trump and the
Oligarchs, elect Progressives Champions, and get Big Money out of
elections!

★ ★ ★

NEW MERCH

As we gear up to protest against MAGA Oligarchs in the Hamptons this
Labor Day, we thought it was a great time to launch our new
sticker:

Donate at least $5 bucks to help power our collective action to
defund Trump and his billionaire cronies — while electing
progressives across the country.

[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/33970?t=1031&akid=9295%2E5885482%2EVrteBy ]GET YOUR STICKER! And thank you for your support.

★ ★ ★

[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/13011?t=1032&akid=9295%2E5885482%2EVrteBy ]WHEN WE ORGANIZE, WE WIN!

★ ★ ★

--------------------------------------------------------------------------

[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/9?t=1001&akid=9295%2E5885482%2EVrteBy ]Facebook | [ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/10?t=1002&akid=9295%2E5885482%2EVrteBy ]Twitter | [ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/11?t=1003&akid=9295%2E5885482%2EVrteBy ]Instagram | [ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/2355?t=1004&akid=9295%2E5885482%2EVrteBy ]Youtube | [ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/2356?t=1005&akid=9295%2E5885482%2EVrteBy ]Website |
[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/10247?t=1006&akid=9295%2E5885482%2EVrteBy ]Sign Up

[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/13119?c0=revreport20250830.d4CImP&t=1007&akid=9295%2E5885482%2EVrteBy ]Our membership swag

Our Revolution is the largest entirely grassroots funded progressive
group in America.

[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/13120?c0=revreport20250830.UqGS1y&t=1008&akid=9295%2E5885482%2EVrteBy ]Help us defend democracy and take the fight directly to the
establishment by committing to a recurring donation of just $5 or more a
month!

[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/13121?c0=revreport20250830.THFMsX&t=1009&akid=9295%2E5885482%2EVrteBy ]GIVE MONTHLY

[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/13122?c0=revreport20250830.EtvKmZ&t=1010&akid=9295%2E5885482%2EVrteBy ]GIVE ONCE

You can unsubscribe from this mailing list at any time:
http://act.ourrevolution.com/cms/unsubscribe/unsubscribe-2/?akid=9295%2E5885482%2EVrteBy

Paid for by Our Revolution click here to unsubscribe

PAID FOR BY OUR REVOLUTION
PO Box 66208
WASHINGTON, DC, 20035

Not authorized by any candidate or candidate committee. Our Revolution is a
501(c)(4) organization. Donations to Our Revolution are not deductible as
charitable contributions for Federal income tax purposes. All donations are
made to support Our Revolution’s general mission and are not designated for
any specific activity.

The electorate is angry about their standard of living declining and about the elites controlling D.C. and enriching themselves,” Our Revolution’s Joseph Geevarghese told the [ ]LA Times. To that end, we’re asking Our Revolution supporters like you to help us shape our candidate endorsement process. Take our [ ]Candidate Priorities Survey to weigh in on what YOU want to see them run on to WIN in the Midterms. ★ ★ ★ [21]Action Center ★ ★ ★ New Yorkers: This is not the time to sit idle. Hop on a bus Sunday, August 31st and join the Day of Resistance in the Hamptons as we protest Oligarchs at the beach! We’re bringing the fight to the MAGA 1%. [ ]Join us here. Our Mission: Fight Trump and the Oligarchy. We have built a powerful network of organizers and activists — 60,000+ connected across the country — and we’re getting to work! [ ]Use this sign-up tool to plug in — and pick your top areas of interest. Whether it’s pressuring lawmakers in their office or amplifying the message online, there are tons of ways to help! ★ ★ ★ [ ]RSVP now for our next National Organize to Win Call on Monday, September 15th! We’ll be discussing our work to fight Trump and the Oligarchs, elect Progressives Champions, and get Big Money out of elections! ★ ★ ★ NEW MERCH As we gear up to protest against MAGA Oligarchs in the Hamptons this Labor Day, we thought it was a great time to launch our new sticker: Donate at least $5 bucks to help power our collective action to defund Trump and his billionaire cronies — while electing progressives across the country. [ ]GET YOUR STICKER! And thank you for your support. ★ ★ ★ [ ]WHEN WE ORGANIZE, WE WIN! ★ ★ ★ [ ]Facebook | [ ]Twitter | [ ]Instagram | [ ]Youtube | [ ]Website | [ ]Sign Up [ ]Our membership swag Our Revolution is the largest entirely grassroots funded progressive group in America. [ ]Help us defend democracy and take the fight directly to the establishment by committing to a recurring donation of just $5 or more a month! body:
| | [ ](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/u001.iIBo0N5fSFzEFL40Tn-vtbMETCUYClkOrOh9-SNJfqsxMJiGPyPf05VFiXvz2rBCXbURlnmnoi6ylJOshf0_SqORUwQxh_Oug38BDDJZa-9I5b9LSISJBCsluxSWtcnxkUfkQzAwggaOaJGJu1aePX09IasJqDqzFerIKkEEwdm8Y9GVYPRn_UHrthsLkKwmfFVPkpYSUGI97aK9p2t_LJs7ROwgG86TOyKpc_be_nIrKMwby22PoIG-y_jvkmY4_MzOVSz0WiuuS2n8fUrxvV7wbKsR9Up6SbGr-aMUFh0XKKgcA8buWxLBOtpvw9thFtQvUJCN1r9jBnXhjpWDRgcJ4tmG385qlB3hFWwI1es/4jh/QNDqhmDJSoSCp7DbVySXDA/h0/h001.qMsT4w5DxDV5OpqT9QSCYIFvq31e8wz26kGahtBlbno)

This is Adam Schiff. I have some important news to share with you, Friends: **Attorney General Pam Bondi has reportedly asked a corrupt DOJ official named Ed Martin to lead a baseless investigation against me.**
---
| [ ](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/u001.iIBo0N5fSFzEFL40Tn-vtbMETCUYClkOrOh9-SNJfqsxMJiGPyPf05VFiXvz2rBCXbURlnmnoi6ylJOshf0_SqORUwQxh_Oug38BDDJZa--UbAGoQ6Z8TJ45HZGaHljZWelDE8LkGYnsI_7duwsWH6b5EugLbXRHKmQYk47qVAX8KEBnWypfBOdUYszVpCr2AhUhPWLMreGCVsv9JJhTujE7-VOVMCiN-QykHEeTdTi919S-VxeD3n_j3fBXVzK2V6F_BdCYOqopuMkd_GR9jV8ph8bCMvjcmWY7ue7QJBhSRa-161jZ04Sx50MB9CoXJm-LVD2CvVv4oIfc0BjACGcU_4Y7s3gZVZMZs6K0tpY/4jh/QNDqhmDJSoSCp7DbVySXDA/h1/h001.wU1zpxaXncEIJDFDEpqgbBmCV9SeyTYDk3CiE8DEcsg)
---
Ed Martin, if you don't remember, was the Interim U.S. Attorney for the District of Columbia. A former defense lawyer for violent insurrectionists, he was patently unfit for that office -- or any other -- and I put a hold on his nomination, and successfully helped kill it. Ed Martin is exactly the type of loyal attack dog Donald Trump adores. As Interim U.S. Attorney, he threatened perceived "enemies" of the Trump administration, fired January 6th prosecutors for simply doing their jobs, urged the opening of criminal cases without evidence, and used the powers of his office to deter free speech. Career prosecutors quit rather than follow his unethical edicts. Before he was nominated to be a U.S. Attorney, Ed Martin had zero prosecutorial experience. Instead, he spent his time defending insurrectionists who broke into the Capitol on January 6 and advancing bogus claims of election fraud. He is close friends with an avowed neo-Nazi, appearing with and praising him repeatedly. _Because of all of that, we were able to stop Ed Martin 's dangerous nomination. _ When his nomination was defeated, Attorney General Pam Bondi appointed Martin to lead a "weaponization" task force at the Justice Department to go after the president's enemies. I was at the top of the list. **And now, Ed Martin is investigating me and carrying out not just the president 's political retribution, but his own, as well. ** [**Friends, this is going to get ugly. But I will not be deterred from speaking out, fighting back, and holding Trump accountable every single day. Will you have my back and split a contribution between me and No Dem Left Behind**** to help me****fight back against Ed Martin and his sham investigation?**](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/u001.iIBo0N5fSFzEFL40Tn-vtbMETCUYClkOrOh9-SNJfqsxMJiGPyPf05VFiXvz2rBCXbURlnmnoi6ylJOshf0_SqORUwQxh_Oug38BDDJZa-_ox1XmNEih20Je_omGM0Hu_tNp-D8ZGdaVUaKDeNk1sgu88Cw53QNYM6fA1WerP-t9rHoPnoQonQpM-CdW6dXjz3meHUlTyNaGUuM0ChLdlbI9jYN6cI3cEQzaqD9UMp4O-vQlBNRL5J182P0GawrODCJNPOgPkRYJpkLVJWXHNMXT5l5ZMlyXzYjGOlA4GzzCmpc_iD_tHMdOr8deru9vzv6719PKV21MXgdhwzB9DHSnPM1qK_IP1dHHAS2stn4/4jh/QNDqhmDJSoSCp7DbVySXDA/h2/h001.-6kS2gy6Y7c1EbpcnFjJXzdWR3XmNLsS5q53EA2EXMU)

name: Marcy Kaptur
subject: Personally reaching out
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Kaptur for Congress
body:
If you believe in our fight for good jobs, affordable healthcare, a healthy planet, and the right for working people to live with dignity...
Hi there,
I wouldn't normally reach out on a Saturday, but you're part of our team and our people-powered movement, so I wanted you to hear this from me:
At midnight tomorrow, we close the books on our monthly fundraising numbers, and we're still behind where we need to be.
With the GOP once again making our district a top target in 2026, these funds are critical. You power our voter registration and mobilization efforts, support our digital outreach, and keep the lights on at our campaign headquarters.
Without our grassroots supporters, we never would have been able to fend off the dark money attacks and defend this seat by less than a percentage point in 2024.
Friend, I wouldn't be asking if it weren't important. If you believe in our fight for good jobs, affordable healthcare, a healthy planet, and the right for working people to live with dignity, will you chip in a few dollars to support our work?
Here's the link: [**http://secure.actblue.com/donate/kaptur25_em?**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/114306236/566591667/-1968428927?=250830_mk_EndAug2_l1&ms=250830_mk_EndAug2&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzMvMS85NTY1NSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI1MDIwMTg2ZC1iNzg1LWYwMTEtYjQ4NC02MDQ1YmRlYjc0MTMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=5oGt0mmKTS9Y_iodYcLyJwl4Wi2Ne1Kar1QRFtKzVDc=)
Thanks so much for standing with me.
— Marcy
Paid for by Kaptur for Congress
Kaptur for Congress
PO Box 899
Toledo, OH 43697
United States

| | [READ IN APP](https://open.substack.com/pub/endlessurgency/p/college-football-is-back-and-im-escaping?utm_source=email&redirect=app-store&utm_campaign=email-read-in-app)
---

I've been fired up all week about the return of college football--something I've loved since I was a kid. Probably a little too much. And this weekend, I'm heading to Miami for a couple of days to take my son and my dad to see Notre Dame (my alma mater) play the University of Miami.
Given how brutal this year's been--between the toxic, soul-sucking political crisis we are fighting through, a personal health scare, and my family's fertility struggles--I've been thinking a lot about escapism. And community. And how both are urgently needed in this relentless fight against the existential threats that have shaken the very foundation of our country.
_**[I'm committed to keeping Endless Urgency free for everyone--but I rely on paid subscribers (starting at just $8/month) to keep it going. If you find value in the work, consider becoming a paid subscriber:](https://substack.com/redirect/451e5465-0bdf-4756-abdd-a8e63953a41f?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**_
[Upgrade to paid](https://substack.com/redirect/451e5465-0bdf-4756-abdd-a8e63953a41f?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
I live for the things, both meaningful and more superficial, that light me up. Hosting an outdoor concert on my block a few weeks ago. Catching a new movie--I can't wait for _Wicked: Part Two_ and the new _Avengers_ movie. A new episode of _Game Changer_ on Dropout (which, by the way, you should absolutely be watching).
Everybody needs a place to retreat to--a pocket of joy. Whether it's to shake off parenting stress with a date night, escape work chaos with an evening walk, or tune out a country that feels colder and crueler by the day, you need somewhere to go. Somewhere to recalibrate, blow off steam, breathe. Somewhere that reminds you how to feel. Sure, escapism can be a trap if you're not rooted in the real world. But when I look back on the hardest chapters of my life, I remember the joy I found in worlds built by brilliant people--stories, games, experiences, voices, characters. That joy helped me hold on. That joy helped me tend to grief. Those worlds helped me reconnect with my people.
For me, college football is one of those worlds. The passion. The pageantry. The strategy. The grit. These kids pour their hearts into the game, and there's something sacred about that. In another life, if I hadn't struggled with my weight growing up, I think I would've tried to coach. There's just nothing like being part of a team's story. Last year, I followed Notre Dame across the country during their playoff run, took my son to two playoff games, and found myself wrapped up in a community that held me up at a time when things felt really hard--especially after we lost the last election.
_**[I'm committed to keeping Endless Urgency free for everyone--but I rely on paid subscribers (starting at just $8/month) to keep it going. If you find value in the work, consider becoming a paid subscriber:](https://substack.com/redirect/451e5465-0bdf-4756-abdd-a8e63953a41f?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**_
[Upgrade to paid](https://substack.com/redirect/451e5465-0bdf-4756-abdd-a8e63953a41f?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
When I quit my job to start Authentic and I felt alone at work, it was the McElroy Brothers and their dumb D&D podcast--_The Adventure Zone_. When my son was born and I was struggling to process becoming a new parent, it was _Red Dead Redemption_ and the Nationals winning the World Series. When I moved to Chicago and felt lost in a new city in the middle of the pandemic, it was _Critical Role_.
You don't have to like the stuff I like. Find what lights you up. You've probably heard me say it before: touch some grass. Maybe you've seen me kayaking or swimming on social media--finally doing the things I love now that I've lost hundreds of pounds and can use this body the way I want. Hiking. Hitting golf balls with my son. Taking my family to Montana this summer. It matters. That joy matters.
Because here's the thing: these fascists--especially Donald Trump--they "flood the zone" to drown us in misery, confusion, and self-isolation. Remember that leaning into joy is a necessity to keep fighting.
So do the opposite. Take care of yourself. Watch that "trashy" show with your BFF. I tune in to _WWE Monday Night Raw_ religiously. My wife loves _Love Island_. I can't stand it, but she lives for it--and that's the point.
So indulge. Find reprieve in anything that makes you laugh, cry, engage, feel. Just don't stop living your life. Don't forget to find your people. Your hobbies. Small rituals that help you tune out the bullshit and remember who you are. Escapism, done right, is resistance. Rest is resistance. Joy is resistance. Reclaiming your energy and being inspired again--that's the fuel that gets us through.
So go resist this holiday weekend. Be with people you love. Hit a cookout. Watch some football. Go protest or do some community service. Do the thing that makes you feel most like you. Because we all need something to escape into or give our lives meaning--and we all have a role to play in the world we're trying to survive and rebuild.
For me this weekend, it's taking my son to the beach today and then tomorrow cheering on the Notre Dame Fighting Irish as they (hopefully) stomp the Miami Hurricanes. I can't wait to lift my son in the air with my dad as we do push-ups for every Notre Dame touchdown.
Go Irish. 🍀

| **Peter, no one expected someone like me to be elected to Congress. But against all odds, I’m here – and it’s important you understand _how_ I got here.** This email is long, so if you don’t have a spare minute to read this all the way through, [**_please consider chipping in a few bucks before my End of Month fundraising deadline tomorrow – so I can keep fighting for all of us in Congress. >>_**](http://clicksp.frostforcongress.com/f/a/RnlpQMa0OiLzNjL6FNffug~~/AAPPABA~/TQIonc-9I7iK4XyS3wbKMsTGV2TdKiD17aQegDdF73QJc1fBhhCOXhljjon9IJrqDqwJT2R-Ijqm_5s5EdVMJbDYHeFwr9Yi2rdWkFNLcDpVPXdhpP4SFOLUzJw8BrFwznqH2NkKPAsdayCrtD-GGwvXsGUlB51stzOHX20IaSxdKa8MHvXHbm9vo6_SZi0SVMFJLv1wB41MyQ98MFKzWKY37RF1gHngY-MBjeu-CiqcReH86Nswc3wx_xZwaCXnv2sRgxJKZMqCBsz0tl8gW6vg6BWMAgyEJzKdiCM0OAScB9PUQfHAKii3VCjjghJaTVoRfLLqtF2iCVR9vT-D-f9EqFZJkCEEY6kviwuBbLc~) I was raised in a working-class family. My parents struggled through more broken systems – from underfunded schools, to outrageous healthcare costs, to juggling different jobs to pay the bills. Even when I grew up and ran for Congress, I was a Gen Z working class kid – I didn’t have the same advantages that other candidates have. I drove for Uber and racked up credit card debt just to make ends meet during my campaign. Because of that, everyone counted me out. **But I was able to defy the odds: by becoming a community leader, the National Organizing Director for March for our Lives, and the first member of Gen Z elected to Congress.** _Now, I need you to help me defy the odds once again to**stay** in Congress._ I’m determined to keep fighting for working-class folks, but I can’t do that without your grassroots support. [**_I’m still $7,954 short of what I need to raise before my End of Month deadline, and we only have until tomorrow. clean_body: | ~ | Peter, no one expected someone like me to be elected to Congress. But against all odds, I’m here – and it’s important you understand how I got here. This email is long, so if you don’t have a spare minute to read this all the way through, ~ I was raised in a working-class family. My parents struggled through more broken systems – from underfunded schools, to outrageous healthcare costs, to juggling different jobs to pay the bills. Even when I grew up and ran for Congress, I was a Gen Z working class kid – I didn’t have the same advantages that other candidates have. I drove for Uber and racked up credit card debt just to make ends meet during my campaign. Because of that, everyone counted me out. But I was able to defy the odds: by becoming a community leader, the National Organizing Director for March for our Lives, and the first member of Gen Z elected to Congress. Now, I need you to help me defy the odds once again tostay in Congress. I’m determined to keep fighting for working-class folks, but I can’t do that without your grassroots support. ~ If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: | | | | | | | ~~ Thanks for hearing me out, Maxwell Frost | ~ Maxwell Alejandro Frost is an organizer representing Florida's 10th Congressional District. In 2022, he made history to become the first member of Gen Z elected to Congress. Maxwell isn't taking any corporate PAC, big sugar, or fossil fuel contributions. He needs your help to protect this seat for poor and working-class people. Will you join our historic movement today? | ~~ ~~ ~~ ~~ ~~ | PAID FOR BY MAXWELL FROST FOR CONGRESS Maxwell Frost for Congress PO Box 772671 Orlando, FL 32877-2671 United States | Email is an important way for us to communicate with our supporters. # [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/114307547/566595798/-550958566?refcode=em-header&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85MjQwNyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJkZDU5OWQ3ZS1iYTg1LWYwMTEtYjQ4NC02MDQ1YmRlYjc0MTMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=lz59ULFe7eDzYhHfP5DOe1r8VW3tqu7Bmnk9gkspNdw=)
[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/114307552/566595802/-550958566?refcode=em-8.30.25-all-a&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85MjQwNyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJkZDU5OWQ3ZS1iYTg1LWYwMTEtYjQ4NC02MDQ1YmRlYjc0MTMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=lz59ULFe7eDzYhHfP5DOe1r8VW3tqu7Bmnk9gkspNdw=)

I know you have received a lot of messages from me and my team lately – thank you for sticking with me.

Asking my supporters like you to donate is my least favorite part of this job. But the challenges we’re up against make it necessary to hold this seat and give Democrats a fighting chance at flipping the House majority next year.

**[If you already understand the stakes in this race, will you chip in $3 before tomorrow’s deadline?](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/114307559/566595808/-550958566?refcode=em-8.30.25-all-a&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85MjQwNyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJkZDU5OWQ3ZS1iYTg1LWYwMTEtYjQ4NC02MDQ1YmRlYjc0MTMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=lz59ULFe7eDzYhHfP5DOe1r8VW3tqu7Bmnk9gkspNdw=)** If you want to learn more, please keep reading this message.

I’ve seen a lot during my time in Congress. Since I was sworn in after flipping this seat in 2022, I’ve made an effort to work with both sides of the aisle to get results for IL-13 and beyond.

So believe me when I tell you that it’s never been more difficult to achieve meaningful progress than right now. House Republicans are bending to this administration at every turn – gutting Medicaid, SNAP, and other essential programs by nearly a trillion dollars in exchange for giving the ultra-wealthy a tax break.

**Despite the odds, I’m not backing down. Not when thousands of working families in Central and Southern Illinois depend on me to fight for them.**

While I have gotten results including **[state education funding](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/114307564/566595815/-1547874?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85MjQwNyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJkZDU5OWQ3ZS1iYTg1LWYwMTEtYjQ4NC02MDQ1YmRlYjc0MTMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=lz59ULFe7eDzYhHfP5DOe1r8VW3tqu7Bmnk9gkspNdw=&emci=d9599d7e-ba85-f011-b484-6045bdeb7413&emdi=dd599d7e-ba85-f011-b484-6045bdeb7413&ceid=13891387)** and legislation to **[protect our veterans](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/114307568/566595820/-612397493?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85MjQwNyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJkZDU5OWQ3ZS1iYTg1LWYwMTEtYjQ4NC02MDQ1YmRlYjc0MTMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=lz59ULFe7eDzYhHfP5DOe1r8VW3tqu7Bmnk9gkspNdw=&emci=d9599d7e-ba85-f011-b484-6045bdeb7413&emdi=dd599d7e-ba85-f011-b484-6045bdeb7413&ceid=13891387)** , it’s no secret that Democrats need to take the House majority to put an end to the MAGA agenda.

That’s what makes my race so critical. I flipped this seat from red to blue before, and it’s kept me a top target. I’m facing multiple challengers in this race who would act as nothing more than a rubber stamp for the national GOP.

**[Your support today, whether you chip in $3 or even $1, will help us build the groundwork to keep this seat blue. Will you consider chipping in before tomorrow’s deadline?](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/114307571/566595823/-550958566?refcode=em-8.30.25-all-a&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85MjQwNyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJkZDU5OWQ3ZS1iYTg1LWYwMTEtYjQ4NC02MDQ1YmRlYjc0MTMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=lz59ULFe7eDzYhHfP5DOe1r8VW3tqu7Bmnk9gkspNdw=)**

clean_body: | = = I know you have received a lot of messages from me and my team lately – thank you for sticking with me. Asking my supporters like you to donate is my least favorite part of this job. But the challenges we’re up against make it necessary to hold this seat and give Democrats a fighting chance at flipping the House majority next year. = If you want to learn more, please keep reading this message. I’ve seen a lot during my time in Congress. Since I was sworn in after flipping this seat in 2022, I’ve made an effort to work with both sides of the aisle to get results for IL-13 and beyond. So believe me when I tell you that it’s never been more difficult to achieve meaningful progress than right now. House Republicans are bending to this administration at every turn – gutting Medicaid, SNAP, and other essential programs by nearly a trillion dollars in exchange for giving the ultra-wealthy a tax break. Despite the odds, I’m not backing down. Not when thousands of working families in Central and Southern Illinois depend on me to fight for them. While I have gotten results including and legislation to , it’s no secret that Democrats need to take the House majority to put an end to the MAGA agenda. That’s what makes my race so critical. I flipped this seat from red to blue before, and it’s kept me a top target. I’m facing multiple challengers in this race who would act as nothing more than a rubber stamp for the national GOP. = If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: | | | = | = | | = | = | | = | = Thank you, Nikki | = = = Nikki is a champion for working families – from leading the fight to pass a $15 minimum wage in Illinois, to bringing manufacturing back to America. Now, she’s delivering in Congress for the people of Illinois' 13th Congressional District. = To learn more about Nikki and her commitment to working families, please visit: = We'd hate to see you go, but you can click here if you'd like to . Ten dollars.

That’s the donation I’m asking you to split between me and Lisa Blunt Rochester before our End of Month Deadline, team. No, that’s not a typo — **just $10**.

$10 to expose Team Extreme’s plans to cut Social Security and Medicare.

$10 to replace the Republican House with a Democratic majority.

$10 to hold extreme MAGA House Republicans accountable for refusing to break from Donald Trump and Elon Musk.

I know $10 might not seem like it can do all those things. But, if everyone gives a little, it adds up quickly. We need 1,000 Democrats to stand up before midnight

| Donald Trump continues to vow to defend Social Security while his actions tell a completely different story:

→ His administration cut nearly 7,000 SSA jobs

→ His cuts forced 6 out of 10 SSA regional offices to close

→ Seniors are left waiting longer for the benefits they’ve earned

**For generations, Social Security has been a lifeline for millions of Americans to retire with dignity – But Trump is putting that promise at risk.**

New Dems are fighting back! Protecting Social Security is one of our top priorities, _because every American deserves a secure future._

* * *
* **TOP NEWS**

**" Federal appeals court to decide whether Texas can give police broad powers to arrest migrants," **via AP**** \-- "A federal appeals court is set to decide whether a [Texas law to give police broad powers](https://substack.com/redirect/8f1523da-0716-4be9-8bc9-17d812a7fd9e?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) to arrest migrants suspected of illegally entering the U.S. can take effect.

The 5th Circuit Court of Appeals on Friday vacated an earlier ruling by a three-judge panel that the law is unconstitutional, and now the full court will consider whether the law can take effect.
[BECOME A PAID SUBSCRIBER: $7/mo](https://substack.com/redirect/83d27ba7-445a-4a2a-b8f3-0c1ea1ad264b?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
[BECOME A PAID SUBSCRIBER: $70/yr](https://substack.com/redirect/83d27ba7-445a-4a2a-b8f3-0c1ea1ad264b?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
The Texas Legislature passed Senate Bill 4 in 2023, but a [federal judge in Texas](https://substack.com/redirect/ffafb525-0a30-49f2-89a7-bdaa47123e5a?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) ruled the law was unconstitutional. Texas then appealed that ruling.

Under the proposed law, state law enforcement officers could arrest people suspected of entering the country illegally. Once in custody, they could agree to a Texas judge's order to leave the country or face a misdemeanor charge for entering the U.S. illegally. Migrants who don't leave after being ordered to do so could be arrested again and charged with a more serious felony.

Texas Gov. **Greg Abbott** said in a social media post on Friday that the court's decision was a "hopeful sign."" [AP](https://substack.com/redirect/6c96acad-822e-4879-9a55-4ff50c9efc47?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
* * *
**PLEASE NOTE** : You are receiving the PAID subscriber version of Must Read Texas -- for TODAY ONLY -- so you can see what you're missing.
Paid subscribers receive story summaries to save time, while free subscribers receive only headlines and links.
» Become a PAID subscriber for $7/mo or $70/yr [here](https://substack.com/redirect/83d27ba7-445a-4a2a-b8f3-0c1ea1ad264b?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).
* * *
* **TOP NEWS**

**" Texas Gov. Greg Abbott signs new congressional redistricting map President Trump requested," **Dallas Morning News'**Aaron Torres** and **Karen Brooks Harper** \-- "Texas Gov. **Greg Abbott** on Friday signed into law a new congressional map that led to a [two-week quorum break](https://substack.com/redirect/52159f03-920e-4299-91f7-c5eff8f77615?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) from dozens of House Democrats, and now that it's in effect could help Republicans maintain control of Congress following next year's midterm elections.

"Texas is now more red in the United States Congress," Abbott said in a video posted on social media of him signing the measure.

The governor did not hold a public bill signing.

President **Donald Trump** asked the Texas Legislature to redraw the state's congressional districts in order to minimize the risk of Republicans losing control of the U.S. House of Representatives in the 2026 elections. House Bill 4, which contains the map, could net five additional seats in Texas for the GOP.

The next battleground for the map is likely to be in federal court after a judge set aside time in [October to consider an injunction](https://substack.com/redirect/42865649-08e0-47d0-b292-af3629219294?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) in an El Paso courtroom.

Abbott called lawmakers back to the Capitol last month for the first of two special sessions to pass the new congressional map and legislation on recovery efforts and safety regulations following catastrophic floods in the Hill Country over July 4 weekend.

Democratic state lawmakers were outraged when the map was introduced late last month, arguing it was racist and would dilute the power of nonwhite voters in Texas. Republicans defended the map, arguing the map was drawn strictly to help the GOP gain seats in Congress.

In an effort to stop the map from passing, more than 50 House Democrats fled the state earlier this month to deny the Texas House a quorum -- freezing all legislative activity -- to stop the map from passing. It didn't work and the map ultimately reached Abbott's desk after the governor said he would keep calling lawmakers back until the congressional districts were redrawn.

Texas Democratic Party Chair**Kendall Scudder** bashed the map in a Friday statement and said the party will continue fighting against it.

"This isn't over -- we'll see these clowns in court," Scudder said in a statement. "We aren't done fighting against these racially discriminatory maps, and fully expect the letter of the law to prevail over these sycophantic Republican politicians who think the rules don't apply to them."

The path to get the map to Abbott's desk drew outrage from different corners of the state -- and has led to a gerrymandering tit-for-tat across the country. When Texas legislative committees held hearings with the public -- in the Capitol and across the state -- an overwhelming majority of individuals who testified spoke out against the map.

However, Republicans said the new map reflects the trends of the voters across Texas, seizing on the rightward shift in some areas of Texas in last year's election. In one instance, a Republican state senator was even more straightforward." [DMN](https://substack.com/redirect/1c0162dc-e40b-42d9-910b-be6764afe47e?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) ($)

**" Camp Mystic parents had to sign a legal release. Can they still sue over flood deaths?" **San Antonio Express-News' **Peggy O'Hare** \-- "Before sending their daughters to Camp Mystic, parents had to sign a waiver giving up their right to sue the camp or its owners if their child was injured or killed.

On July 4, a [massive flood struck the Texas Hill Country, killing 25 campers ](https://substack.com/redirect/8cf834c9-5df5-47de-8f59-ba67974d3870?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)and two counselors at the Christian girls' retreat southwest of Kerrville.

The question now: Will that legal waiver prevent grieving families from suing Camp Mystic for damages?

The answer: No. That's the consensus of experienced civil litigators interviewed by the San Antonio Express-News. Families will have avenues to bring lawsuits, despite the waiver, they said.

Less certain is whether they will be able to secure significant compensation. Proving that the camp's owners committed gross negligence will be difficult, lawyers said, and even a successful legal strategy could confront a harsh financial reality: In the end, there may not be much money to distribute.

"As many barriers as may exist, it will not stop a good faith effort by a parent or a child who was a victim of this horrific situation from making a claim," said **Bruce Steckler** , a personal injury lawyer in Dallas.

"These are children," he said. "These are people who are unable, generally speaking, to care for themselves. That's why they have counselors and camp directors."

**Robert "Robby" Alden**, an Austin lawyer, noted that personal injury suits usually are resolved through negotiated settlements. "The determinant will be how much insurance" the camp has, "and whether there are parents that are so determined to get full compensation that they want to force the camp to sell the property and dissolve," he said.

**Randy Sorrels** , a Houston personal injury lawyer, said it was unlikely Camp Mystic had enough insurance to satisfy plaintiffs' claims.

"Even if you took that property and sold it, what could you get for it when you divide it among the 27 deaths of Camp Mystic?" he said. "It's peanuts. It's pennies."

The camp's real estate has a market value of nearly $15 million, according to the Kerr Central Appraisal District.

**Jeff Ray** , a lawyer for Camp Mystic, said in a statement Friday that since no lawsuit has yet been filed against the camp over the July 4 disaster, "it would be speculation on our part to attempt to set out any defenses."

But Ray's statement went on to say that cabins in the part of the camp known as "the Flats," where all the deaths occurred, were outside the 100-year floodplain as defined by the Federal Emergency Management Agency. Camp Mystic successfully petitioned the agency in 2013 and 2019 to amend its flood maps to recognize that the cabins were at a higher elevation than the floodplain, Ray said.

"The massive flash flooding on July 4, 2025, was an unprecedented event that likely exceeded a 1,000-year floodplain event," his statement said." [SAEN](https://substack.com/redirect/3eea8785-b185-4000-8273-380e1c2cc23c?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) ($)

**" Settlement reached in Texas, New Mexico water dispute," **Inside Climate News' **Martha Pskowski** \-- "The Rio Grande flows over 1,800 miles from the mountains of southwestern Colorado to the Gulf of Mexico. A lawsuit filed in 2013 between Texas and New Mexico over Rio Grande water has taken as many twists and turns as the river itself.

A settlement signed this week by New Mexico, the Department of Justice and two irrigation districts, and reviewed by Inside Climate News, lays out agreements for irrigation management on the Rio Grande. It is one part of a larger settlement package that will be presented to a special master in the case, Judge **D. Brooks Smith** of the U.S. Third Circuit Court of Appeals, for approval next month.

The outcome of the case is expected to have broad implications for cities that rely on the Rio Grande and farmers throughout New Mexico and far west Texas.

The settlement package includes new formulas to calculate how much water each entity is owed; an agreement for New Mexico to reduce groundwater depletion, and changes to the operating manual for the Bureau of Reclamation's Rio Grande Project.

Under the settlement, New Mexico could transfer water rights from the Elephant Butte Irrigation District (EBID) in Southern New Mexico in order to meet its obligations to Texas. The state agrees in the settlement that it would compensate EBID.

The case began when Texas alleged that groundwater pumping in Southern New Mexico deprives the state of water it is owed under the Rio Grande Compact. Colorado and the United States are also parties to the case. Local irrigation districts, cities and agricultural interest groups have been involved as friends of the court. The case has evolved from a dispute between Texas and New Mexico to encompass conflicts between groundwater and surface water users in the area.

"We are ecstatic to have reached a settlement and look forward to continue delivering water to our farmers and the City of El Paso," said **Jay Ornelas** , general manager of the El Paso Water Improvement District No. 1, an irrigation district. "The agreement provides long-term protection to El Paso farmers and the City of El Paso that rely on water from the federal Rio Grande Project."" [Inside Climate News](https://substack.com/redirect/367692ad-cde7-4de8-811f-020147573c72?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)

* **#TXLEGE**

**" THC ban stalls out down the homestretch of Texas special legislative session," **Houston Chronicle's **Isaac Yu** \-- "After several months of fiery debate and tearful testimonies over the prospect of banning THC statewide, proposed measures to do so have stalled in the Texas House.

[Senate Bill 6](https://substack.com/redirect/f123d2e6-98ad-4cc8-a3c2-391511676fb3?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), which would have created a blanket ban on products containing any "detectable amount of any cannabinoid" other than cannabidiol and cannabigerol, better known as CBD and CBG, non-intoxicating components of cannabis, hasn't been heard in a House committee after the Senate passed it Aug. 19. The House's version of the bill hasn't been heard in its chamber's committee either.

Ten days might not be long for a bill to sit dormant during a regular legislative session, but with state leadership suggesting that the current special legislative session could wind down in the coming days, lawmakers would have to move fast on THC upon reconvening after Monday's holiday.

Without further regulations or a ban being discussed by lawmakers in the House, the most likely scenario is that hemp-derived THC remains legal in Texas, but with more enforcement of current laws restricting the drug.

"It seems like a lot of people don't want anything to do with it," said **Lukas Gilkey** , chief executive of Hometown Hero, an Austin-based manufacturer of hemp-derived THC products. "It's a hot potato."" [Texas Tribune](https://substack.com/redirect/95305c22-97c2-495b-9334-9e7a24fd5cb8?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)

**" Kerr County youth camps appeal to Dan Patrick on proposed floodplain restrictions," **Houston Chronicle's **Isaac Yu** \-- "The owners of three Kerr County youth camps have asked Lt. Gov. **Dan Patrick** to reconsider some of the stricter new flood safety requirements contained in two bills before the Texas Legislature that have been filed as a result of the tragic July 4 Guadalupe River flooding that killed 27 Camp Mystic campers.

Two camp safety bills, [House Bill 1](https://substack.com/redirect/6f776ed4-a7ed-4846-9488-c901c3a3ae37?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) and [Senate Bill 1](https://substack.com/redirect/3bcd1eb3-f03b-4a95-88f8-ab510e73328f?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), would withhold state licensing if cabins are located in a floodplain. A week ago, both bills were [passed](https://substack.com/redirect/c13904ab-ee49-4a62-8398-dd3c60d5b84e?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) by their respective home chambers.

Late Friday, The Texas Tribune obtained an Aug. 28 letter sent to Patrick by the owners of Camp Waldemar, Vista Camps and Camp Stewart. In it, the camp owners cite the cost of rebuilding cabins and ask Patrick to "work with us" by having an expert to propose "a safe and professionally analyzed solution through the Texas Water Development Board for the 100-year floodplain prohibition."

The camp owners also insisted that there "must be meaningful financial support, whether through insurance, state grants, or other funding mechanisms, so that the burden does not fall solely on families, camps, and communities."

State Sen. **Charles Perry** , R-Lubbock, who chairs the Senate Select Committee on Disaster Preparedness and Flood and the primary author of one of the bills, told the Tribune last week there would be no state assistance for camps to comply with pending legislation if it passes.

"No, camps are private enterprises," Perry told The Texas Tribune [after family members of the 27 Camp Mystic flood victims](https://substack.com/redirect/634b3d8b-4a73-40f7-b7e2-21b3981505fd?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) testified before his committee on Aug. 20. "The state's not rebuilding private sector camps."

The Texas Tribune reached out to Patrick's office for comment on the letter, which was also forwarded to members of the Texas Senate and Gov. **Greg Abbott** , and did not get an immediate response. The Tribune left phone messages at all three camps, asking for more detail and comment on the letter, but none were returned. The Tribune reached **Meg Clark** , executive director of Camp Waldemar late Friday. She confirmed the contents of the letter but declined to offer additional comment.

The two special legislative committees appointed after the July 4 disaster so far in public hearings have resisted discussing restricting development in floodplains statewide. Requiring camps to move cabins out of the floodplain was the biggest step they had taken in that direction -- and is a major piece of the legislation. Flooding experts say getting kids out of risky areas as they sleep is a clear way to help protect them.

Originally, legislators had planned just to require that camps evacuate kids from campgrounds in the floodplain if the weather service issued a flash flood warning and to install ladders on cabins so campers could climb onto rooftops if the situation grew dire and for some reason they hadn't evacuated. But parents of the kids who died at Camp Mystic pushed to get more restrictive, camp-focused legislation on the table.

"The combination of devastating floods and the heavy financial burden proposed under new state regulations presents an impossible challenge," the camps' letter stated. "Collectively, our camps would face millions of dollars in mandated rebuilding costs for cabins subjected to the prohibition that did not sustain damage by recent flooding. These additional burdens would come on top of already significant flood repairs, operational expenses, and existing loans."

A representative for the Camp Mystic families' campaign for camp safety said, "We believe the parents' testimonies and recent media interviews speak for themselves. We have no comment about this letter, but we support lawmakers' efforts to pass SB1 and HB1 to ensure common sense safety reforms are in place for the 2026 summer camp season."" [Texas Tribune](https://substack.com/redirect/87a58336-7d72-4b3b-97cd-189f6ad2465b?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)

**" Longtime Houston state Rep. Alma Allen announces retirement, endorses son for her seat," **Houston Chronicle's **Isaac Yu** \-- "State Rep. **Alma Allen** of Houston announced Thursday she plans to retire next year, [endorsing her son's campaign for her district](https://substack.com/redirect/5056ad07-b4a3-4aee-9521-f3ce58bc512c?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).

The 10-term Democrat is one of the most senior members in her party, representing a deep-blue swath of southern Harris County, including parts of Alief and Hiram Clarke. Prior to her election to the Texas House in 2004, Allen served on the State Board of Education and worked as a teacher and administrator in Houston ISD.

Allen said she would formally announce her retirement to her colleagues on the House floor on Wednesday, when the Legislature is expected to adjourn from the ongoing special session.

"I'm proud of what I have accomplished, and it's just time for me to move on to another phase of my life," Allen said in a brief interview Thursday.

Across 10 terms, Allen leaned into her experience on the House Public Education Committee, playing a role in passing a sweeping school finance package in 2019. She also previously served as vice chair on the Corrections Committee. In her last session, Allen fought against the passage of private school vouchers.

Her son, **Lawrence Allen, Jr**., has yet to file for his mother's seat. His career path is similar to hers: he worked as a Houston ISD teacher and administrator before being elected to the State Board of Education, serving for 15 years before stepping down in 2023.

He also ran for Texas House in 2020 for a competitive Fort Bend County district, coming fourth in the Democratic primary. That election was ultimately won by Republican**Jacey Jetton**.

"I caught the political bug watching and helping my mother over the years," the younger Allen said. "She and I have been talking about this for quite some time, and certainly we wanted to make sure she was ready. And now she's ready."" [Hou Chron](https://substack.com/redirect/8562d5bd-48da-449a-8eae-ade7adca56d1?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) ($)
* * *
* **2026**

**" State Rep. John Lujan Files for Newly Drawn 35th Congressional District," **The Texan's **Cameron Abrams** \-- "State Rep. **John Lujan** (R-San Antonio) is planning to make the jump from Austin to Washington, D.C., filing to run for the newly created Texas Congressional District (CD) 35 next year.

The newly [redrawn](https://substack.com/redirect/2540aec6-d0d7-4be0-936b-90c531c4811b?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) congressional districts have shifted a number of Democratic-heavy districts to being solidly Republican. One of those is CD 35, currently held by Congressman **Greg Casar** (D-TX-35), who has already said he will be shifting his focus to run in CD 37. That seat is currently occupied by Congressman **Lloyd Doggett**(D-TX-37), who [announced](https://substack.com/redirect/8b2cb161-ca01-44b0-b747-d6b784c6b401?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) he would not run again in the new version of the district. According to The Texan's [Texas Partisan Index](https://substack.com/redirect/00f56aa1-d76f-48a8-91ba-30df9c3725c0?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), CD 35 moves from D-70% to R-55%.

Lujan [told](https://substack.com/redirect/ede93a06-9934-4654-90f7-c4cc38764eba?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) the San Antonio Report that "You don't get an opportunity like this very often."

"I'm big into service, and what a great opportunity to answer the call."

The path to elected office for Lujan has not always been easy. In 2016, he first landed in the House District (HD) 118 seat after a special election when Democratic incumbent state Rep. **Joe Farias** resigned. Lujan served the remainder of the term before losing to Democrat **Tomas Uresti i** n 2016, then made an unsuccessful bid to reclaim it in 2018.

Lujan later secured HD 118 in a runoff during a [special election](https://substack.com/redirect/c7bd3bee-f84d-466c-b60d-e06c806796c3?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) in 2021, narrowly defeating Democrat **Frank Ramirez**. He was [re-relected](https://substack.com/redirect/079b7560-043d-47fb-98e1-551809def8cf?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) in 2024, besting Democrat **Kristian Carranza** in one of the most competitive state House races.

CD 35 is one of the five districts targeted by Texas Republicans for a mid-decade redistricting flip, a move requested by President**Donald Trump**. The seat currently held by Casar was moved out of Austin and down near San Antonio, flipping it from deep blue to solidly Republican.

Since becoming a member of the Texas House, Lujan has found himself assigned to a number of committees, including Appropriations and Trade, Workforce, and Economic Development. During the 89th Legislative Session, he voted in favor of school choice through education savings accounts and legislation to [decriminalize homosexual conduct](https://substack.com/redirect/2542eaf4-a5e5-4054-a528-1a61ee9e765d?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) in state code.

Lujan is not the only one announcing they will seek the CD 35 seat in 2026. **Christopher Schuchardt** , who narrowly lost his race for Bexar County commissioner last year, has filed to run, and San Antonio District 10 City Councilman **Marc Whyte** and Texas Funeral Service Commission appointee **Kristin Tips** have also expressed [interest](https://substack.com/redirect/1fee521b-fe35-48b6-bc40-a69958a9c079?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).

The HD 118 seat is now in search of a replacement. The R-51% San Antonio-centric district has already seen tight races with 2026 sure to be another heated battle. Carranza had already announced a return bid for the seat, and now Republicans must find a new candidate to try and keep the seat." [The Texan](https://substack.com/redirect/84d971d2-cf43-4a45-bc28-f64907d500b6?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
